construct_instance(form, instance, fields=None, exclude=None):\\n51:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n52:     Construct and return a model instance from the bound ``form``'s\\n53:     ``cleaned_data``, but do not save the returned instance to the database.\\n54:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n55:     from django.db import models\\n56: \\n57:     opts = instance._meta\\n58: \\n59:     cleaned_data = form.cleaned_data\\n60:     file_field_list = []\\n61:     for f in opts.fields:\\n62:         if (\\n63:             not f.editable\\n64:             or isinstance(f, models.AutoField)\\n65:             or f.name not in cleaned_data\\n66:         ):\\n67:             continue\\n68:         if fields is not None and f.name not in fields:\\n69:             continue\\n70:         if exclude and f.name in exclude:\\n71:             continue\\n72:         # Leave defaults for fields that aren't in POST data, except for\\n73:         # checkbox inputs because they don't appear in POST data if not\\n74:         # checked.\\n75:         if (\\n76:             f.has_default()\\n77:             and form[f.name].field.widget.value_omitted_from_data(\\n78:                 form.data, form.files, form.add_prefix(f.name)\\n79:             )\\n80:             and cleaned_data.get(f.name) in form[f.name].field.empty_values\\n81:         ):\\n82:             continue\\n83:         # Defer saving file-type fields until after the other fields, so a\\n84:         # callable upload_to can use the values from other fields.\\n85:         if isinstance(f, models.FileField):\\n86:             file_field_list.append(f)\\n87:         else:\\n88:             f.save_form_data(instance, cleaned_data[f.name])\\n89: \\n90:     for f in file_field_list:\\n91:         f.save_form_data(instance, cleaned_data[f.name])\\n92: \\n93:     return instance\\n94: \\n95: \\n96: # ModelForms #################################################################\\n97: \\n98: \\n99: def \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/forms/models.py%3FL1-100\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: \"\"\"\n2: Helper functions for creating Form classes from Django models\n3: and database field objects.\n4: \"\"\"\n5: \n6: from itertools import chain\n7: \n8: from django.core.exceptions import (\n9:     NON_FIELD_ERRORS,\n10:     FieldError,\n11:     ImproperlyConfigured,\n12:     ValidationError,\n13: )\n14: from django.core.validators import ProhibitNullCharactersValidator\n15: from django.db.models.utils import AltersData\n16: from django.forms.fields import ChoiceField, Field\n17: from django.forms.forms import BaseForm, DeclarativeFieldsMetaclass\n18: from django.forms.formsets import BaseFormSet, formset_factory\n19: from django.forms.utils import ErrorList\n20: from django.forms.widgets import (\n21:     HiddenInput,\n22:     MultipleHiddenInput,\n23:     RadioSelect,\n24:     SelectMultiple,\n25: )\n26: from django.utils.choices import BaseChoiceIterator\n27: from django.utils.hashable import make_hashable\n28: from django.utils.text import capfirst, get_text_list\n29: from django.utils.translation import gettext\n30: from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _\n31: \n32: __all__ = (\n33:     \"ModelForm\",\n34:     \"BaseModelForm\",\n35:     \"model_to_dict\",\n36:     \"fields_for_model\",\n37:     \"ModelChoiceField\",\n38:     \"ModelMultipleChoiceField\",\n39:     \"ALL_FIELDS\",\n40:     \"BaseModelFormSet\",\n41:     \"modelformset_factory\",\n42:     \"BaseInlineFormSet\",\n43:     \"inlineformset_factory\",\n44:     \"modelform_factory\",\n45: )\n46: \n47: ALL_FIELDS = \"__all__\"\n48: \n49: \n50: def construct_instance(form, instance, fields=None, exclude=None):\n51:     \"\"\"\n52:     Construct and return a model instance from the bound ``form``'s\n53:     ``cleaned_data``, but do not save the returned instance to the database.\n54:     \"\"\"\n55:     from django.db import models\n56: \n57:     opts = instance._meta\n58: \n59:     cleaned_data = form.cleaned_data\n60:     file_field_list = []\n61:     for f in opts.fields:\n62:         if (\n63:             not f.editable\n64:             or isinstance(f, models.AutoField)\n65:             or f.name not in cleaned_data\n66:         ):\n67:             continue\n68:         if fields is not None and f.name not in fields:\n69:             continue\n70:         if exclude and f.name in exclude:\n71:             continue\n72:         # Leave defaults for fields that aren't in POST data, except for\n73:         # checkbox inputs because they don't appear in POST data if not\n74:         # checked.\n75:         if (\n76:             f.has_default()\n77:             and form[f.name].field.widget.value_omitted_from_data(\n78:                 form.data, form.files, form.add_prefix(f.name)\n79:             )\n80:             and cleaned_data.get(f.name) in form[f.name].field.empty_values\n81:         ):\n82:             continue\n83:         # Defer saving file-type fields until after the other fields, so a\n84:         # callable upload_to can use the values from other fields.\n85:         if isinstance(f, models.FileField):\n86:             file_field_list.append(f)\n87:         else:\n88:             f.save_form_data(instance, cleaned_data[f.name])\n89: \n90:     for f in file_field_list:\n91:         f.save_form_data(instance, cleaned_data[f.name])\n92: \n93:     return instance\n94: \n95: \n96: # ModelForms #################################################################\n97: \n98: \n99: def model_to_dict(instance, fields=None, exclude=None):\n100:     \"\"\"\n101: ",

            "content": "1355: \n1356: .. class:: ModelChoiceField(**kwargs)\n1357: \n1358:     * Default widget: :class:`Select`\n1359:     * Empty value: ``None``\n1360: ",

            "endLine": 1333,

      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "47: * Fixed serialization of\n48:   :class:`~django.contrib.postgres.fields.DateRangeField` and\n49:   :class:`~django.contrib.postgres.fields.DateTimeRangeField` (:ticket:`24937`).\n50: \n51: ",
            "endLine": 51,
            "startLine": 47
          },
          {
            "content": "34: * Fixed a regression in 1.8.6 by restoring the ability to use ``Manager``\n35:   objects for the ``queryset`` argument of ``ModelChoiceField``\n36:   (:ticket:`25683`).\n37: ",
            "endLine": 37,
            "startLine": 34
          },
          {
            "content": "40: \n41: * Fixed a data loss possibility with :class:`~django.db.models.Prefetch` if\n42:   ``to_attr`` is set to a ``ManyToManyField`` (:ticket:`25693`).\n43: ",
            "endLine": 43,
            "startLine": 40
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/releases/1.8.7.txt",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c/-/blob/docs/releases/1.8.7.txt"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "544:   during module import, because the ``QuerySet`` is iterated over. To avoid the\n545:   warning, the form could use a :class:`~django.forms.ModelChoiceField`\n546:   instead::\n547: ",
            "endLine": 547,
            "startLine": 544
          },
          {
            "content": "71: \n72: If no :class:`AppConfig` subclass is found, the base :class:`AppConfig` class\n73: will be used.\n74: ",
            "endLine": 74,
            "startLine": 71
          },
          {
            "content": "149: \n150: This example shows project-specific configuration classes located in a\n151: submodule called ``apps.py``. This is a convention, not a requirement.\n152: :class:`AppConfig` subclasses may be defined anywhere.\n153: \n154: ",
            "endLine": 154,
            "startLine": 149
          }
        ],

      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "24: * Fixed a regression in the number of queries when using ``RadioSelect`` with a\n25:   ``ModelChoiceField`` form field (:ticket:`27001`).\n26: \n27: ",
            "endLine": 27,
            "startLine": 24
          },
          {
            "content": "81: \n82: * Reallowed subclassing ``UserCreationForm`` without ``USERNAME_FIELD`` in\n83:   ``Meta.fields`` (:ticket:`27111`).\n84: ",
            "endLine": 84,
            "startLine": 81

          {
            "content": "63: \n64: * Fixed ``contrib.admindocs`` crash when a view is in a class, such as some of\n65:   the admin views (:ticket:`27018`).\n66: ",
            "endLine": 66,
            "startLine": 63
          }
        ],

        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "313: \n314: * :class:`~django.forms.ModelChoiceIterator`, used by\n315:   :class:`~django.forms.ModelChoiceField` and\n316:   :class:`~django.forms.ModelMultipleChoiceField`, now uses\n317:   :class:`~django.forms.ModelChoiceIteratorValue` that can be used by widgets\n318:   to access model instances. See :ref:`iterating-relationship-choices` for\n319: ",
            "endLine": 319,
            "startLine": 313
          },
          {
            "content": "736: \n737: * :class:`~django.forms.ModelChoiceIterator`, used by\n738:   :class:`~django.forms.ModelChoiceField` and\n739:   :class:`~django.forms.ModelMultipleChoiceField`, now yields 2-tuple choices\n740:   containing :class:`~django.forms.ModelChoiceIteratorValue` instances as the\n741:   first ``value`` element in each choice. In most cases this proxies\n742: ",
            "endLine": 742,
            "startLine": 736
          },
          {
            "content": "655: \n656: * ``django.utils.decorators.classproperty()`` decorator is made public and\n657:   moved to :class:`django.utils.functional.classproperty`.\n658: \n659: ",
            "endLine": 659,

            "content": "81: \n82: :class:`DateField`                  :class:`~django.forms.DateField`\n83: \n84: :class:`DateTimeField`              :class:`~django.forms.DateTimeField`\n85: \n86: :class:`DecimalField`               :class:`~django.forms.DecimalField`\n87: \n88: :class:`DurationField`              :class:`~django.forms.DurationField`\n89: \n90: :class:`EmailField`                 :class:`~django.forms.EmailField`\n91: \n92: :class:`FileField`                  :class:`~django.forms.FileField`\n93: \n94: :class:`FilePathField`              :class:`~django.forms.FilePathField`\n95: \n96: :class:`FloatField`                 :class:`~django.forms.FloatField`\n97: \n98: :class:`ForeignKey`                 :class:`~django.forms.ModelChoiceField`\n99:                                     (see below)\n100: ",
            "endLine": 100,

            "content": "745:   however, you must ensure that ``ModelForm`` appears first in the MRO. This is\n746:   because these classes rely on different metaclasses and a class can only have\n747:   one metaclass.\n748: \n749: ",
            "endLine": 749,

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1466: \\n1467: class ModelChoiceField(ChoiceField):\\n1468:     \\\"\\\"\\\"A ChoiceField whose choices are a model QuerySet.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1469: \\n1470:     # This class is a subclass of ChoiceField for purity, but it doesn't\\n1471:     # actually use any of ChoiceField's implementation.\\n1472:     default_error_messages = {\\n1473:         \\\"invalid_choice\\\": _(\\n1474:             \\\"Select a valid choice. That choice is not one of the available choices.\\\"\\n1475:         ),\\n1476:     }\\n1477:     iterator = ModelChoiceIterator\\n1478: \\n1479:     def __init__(\\n1480:         self,\\n1481:         queryset,\\n1482:         *,\\n1483:         empty_label=\\\"---------\\\",\\n1484:         required=True,\\n1485:         widget=None,\\n1486:         label=None,\\n1487:         initial=None,\\n1488:         help_text=\\\"\\\",\\n1489:         to_field_name=None,\\n1490:         limit_choices_to=None,\\n1491:         blank=False,\\n1492:         **kwargs,\\n1493:     ):\\n1494:         # Call Field instead of ChoiceField __init__() because we don't need\\n1495:         # ChoiceField.__init__().\\n1496:         Field.__init__(\\n1497:             self,\\n1498:             required=required,\\n1499:             widget=widget,\\n1500:             label=label,\\n1501:             initial=initial,\\n1502:             help_text=help_text,\\n1503:             **kwargs,\\n1504:         )\\n1505:         if (required and initial is not None) or (\\n1506:             isinstance(self.widget, RadioSelect) and not blank\\n1507:         ):\\n1508:             self.empty_label = None\\n1509:         else:\\n1510:             self.empty_label = empty_label\\n1511:         self.queryset = queryset\\n1512:         self.limit_choices_to = limit_choices_to  # limit the queryset later.\\n1513:         self.to_field_name = to_field_name\\n1514: \\n1515:     def validate_no_null_characters(self, value):\\n1516:         non_null_character_validator = ProhibitNullCharactersValidator()\\n1517:         return non_null_character_validator(value)\\n1518: \\n1519:     def get_limit_choices_to(self):\\n1520:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1521:         Return ``limit_choices_to`` for this form field.\\n1522: \\n1523:         If it is a callable, invoke it and return the result.\\n1524:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1525:         if callable(self.limit_choices_to):\\n1526:             return self.limit_choices_to()\\n1527:         return self.limit_choices_to\\n1528: \\n1529:     def __deepcopy__(self, memo):\\n1530:         result = super(ChoiceField, self).__deepcopy__(memo)\\n1531:         # Need to force a new ModelChoiceIterator to be created, bug #11183\\n1532:         if self.queryset is not None:\\n1533:             result.queryset = self.queryset.all()\\n1534:         return result\\n1535: \\n1536:     def _get_queryset(self):\\n1537:         return self._queryset\\n1538: \\n1539:     def _set_queryset(self, queryset):\\n1540:         self._queryset = None if queryset is None else queryset.all()\\n1541:         self.widget.choices = self.choices\\n1542: \\n1543:     queryset = property(_get_queryset, _set_queryset)\\n1544: \\n1545:     # this method will be used to create object labels by the QuerySetIterator.\\n1546:     # Override it to customize the label.\\n1547:     def label_from_instance(self, obj):\\n1548:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1549:         Convert objects into strings and generate the labels for the choices\\n1550:         presented by this object. Subclasses can override this method to\\n1551:         customize the display of the choices.\\n1552:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1553:         return str(obj)\\n1554: \\n1555:     def _get_choices(self):\\n1556:         # If self._choices is set, then somebody must have manually set\\n1557:         # the property self.choices. In this case, just return self._choices.\\n1558:         if hasattr(self, \\\"_choices\\\"):\\n1559:             return self._choices\\n1560: \\n1561:         # Otherwise, execute the QuerySet in self.queryset to determine the\\n1562:         # choices dynamically. Return a fresh ModelChoiceIterator that has not\\n1563:         # been consumed. Note that we're instantiating a new\\n1564:         # ModelChoiceIterator *each* time _get_choices() is called (and, thus,\\n1565:         # each time self.choices is accessed) so that we can ensure the\\n1566:         # QuerySet has not been consumed. This construct might look complicated\\n1567:         # but it allows for lazy evaluation of the queryset.\\n1568:         return self.iterator(self)\\n1569: \\n1570:     choices = property(_get_choices, ChoiceField.choices.fset)\\n1571: \\n1572:     def prepare_value(self, value):\\n1573:         if hasattr(value, \\\"_meta\\\"):\\n1574:             if self.to_field_name:\\n1575:                 return value.serializable_value(self.to_field_name)\\n1576:             else:\\n1577:                 return value.pk\\n1578:         return super().prepare_value(value)\\n1579: \\n1580:     def to_python(self, value):\\n1581:         if value in self.empty_values:\\n1582:             return None\\n1583:         self.validate_no_null_characters(value)\\n1584:         try:\\n1585:             key = self.to_field_name or \\\"pk\\\"\\n1586:             if isinstance(value, self.queryset.model):\\n1587:                 value = getattr(value, key)\\n1588:             value = self.queryset.get(**{key: value})\\n1589:         except (\\n1590:             ValueError,\\n1591:             TypeError,\\n1592:             self.queryset.model.DoesNotExist,\\n1593:             ValidationError,\\n1594:         ):\\n1595:             raise ValidationError(\\n1596:                 self.error_messages[\\\"invalid_choice\\\"],\\n1597:                 code=\\\"invalid_choice\\\",\\n1598:                 params={\\\"value\\\": value},\\n1599:             )\\n1600:         return value\\n1601: \\n1602:     def validate(self, value):\\n1603:         return Field.validate(self, value)\\n1604: \\n1605:     def has_changed(self, initial, data):\\n1606:         if self.disabled:\\n1607:             return False\\n1608:         initial_value = initial if initial is not None else \\\"\\\"\\n1609:         data_value = data if data is not None else \\\"\\\"\\n1610:         return str(self.prepare_value(initial_value)) != str(data_value)\\n1611: \\n1612: \\n1613: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/forms/models.py%3FL1466-1612\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1466: \n1467: class ModelChoiceField(ChoiceField):\n1468:     \"\"\"A ChoiceField whose choices are a model QuerySet.\"\"\"\n1469: \n1470:     # This class is a subclass of ChoiceField for purity, but it doesn't\n1471:     # actually use any of ChoiceField's implementation.\n1472:     default_error_messages = {\n1473:         \"invalid_choice\": _(\n1474:             \"Select a valid choice. That choice is not one of the available choices.\"\n1475:         ),\n1476:     }\n1477:     iterator = ModelChoiceIterator\n1478: \n1479:     def __init__(\n1480:         self,\n1481:         queryset,\n1482:         *,\n1483:         empty_label=\"---------\",\n1484:         required=True,\n1485:         widget=None,\n1486:         label=None,\n1487:         initial=None,\n1488:         help_text=\"\",\n1489:         to_field_name=None,\n1490:         limit_choices_to=None,\n1491:         blank=False,\n1492:         **kwargs,\n1493:     ):\n1494:         # Call Field instead of ChoiceField __init__() because we don't need\n1495:         # ChoiceField.__init__().\n1496:         Field.__init__(\n1497:             self,\n1498:             required=required,\n1499:             widget=widget,\n1500:             label=label,\n1501:             initial=initial,\n1502:             help_text=help_text,\n1503:             **kwargs,\n1504:         )\n1505:         if (required and initial is not None) or (\n1506:             isinstance(self.widget, RadioSelect) and not blank\n1507:         ):\n1508:             self.empty_label = None\n1509:         else:\n1510:             self.empty_label = empty_label\n1511:         self.queryset = queryset\n1512:         self.limit_choices_to = limit_choices_to  # limit the queryset later.\n1513:         self.to_field_name = to_field_name\n1514: \n1515:     def validate_no_null_characters(self, value):\n1516:         non_null_character_validator = ProhibitNullCharactersValidator()\n1517:         return non_null_character_validator(value)\n1518: \n1519:     def get_limit_choices_to(self):\n1520:         \"\"\"\n1521:         Return ``limit_choices_to`` for this form field.\n1522: \n1523:         If it is a callable, invoke it and return the result.\n1524:         \"\"\"\n1525:         if callable(self.limit_choices_to):\n1526:             return self.limit_choices_to()\n1527:         return self.limit_choices_to\n1528: \n1529:     def __deepcopy__(self, memo):\n1530:         result = super(ChoiceField, self).__deepcopy__(memo)\n1531:         # Need to force a new ModelChoiceIterator to be created, bug #11183\n1532:         if self.queryset is not None:\n1533:             result.queryset = self.queryset.all()\n1534:         return result\n1535: \n1536:     def _get_queryset(self):\n1537:         return self._queryset\n1538: \n1539:     def _set_queryset(self, queryset):\n1540:         self._queryset = None if queryset is None else queryset.all()\n1541:         self.widget.choices = self.choices\n1542: \n1543:     queryset = property(_get_queryset, _set_queryset)\n1544: \n1545:     # this method will be used to create object labels by the QuerySetIterator.\n1546:     # Override it to customize the label.\n1547:     def label_from_instance(self, obj):\n1548:         \"\"\"\n1549:         Convert objects into strings and generate the labels for the choices\n1550:         presented by this object. Subclasses can override this method to\n1551:         customize the display of the choices.\n1552:         \"\"\"\n1553:         return str(obj)\n1554: \n1555:     def _get_choices(self):\n1556:         # If self._choices is set, then somebody must have manually set\n1557:         # the property self.choices. In this case, just return self._choices.\n1558:         if hasattr(self, \"_choices\"):\n1559:             return self._choices\n1560: \n1561:         # Otherwise, execute the QuerySet in self.queryset to determine the\n1562:         # choices dynamically. Return a fresh ModelChoiceIterator that has not\n1563:         # been consumed. Note that we're instantiating a new\n1564:         # ModelChoiceIterator *each* time _get_choices() is called (and, thus,\n1565:         # each time self.choices is accessed) so that we can ensure the\n1566:         # QuerySet has not been consumed. This construct might look complicated\n1567:         # but it allows for lazy evaluation of the queryset.\n1568:         return self.iterator(self)\n1569: \n1570:     choices = property(_get_choices, ChoiceField.choices.fset)\n1571: \n1572:     def prepare_value(self, value):\n1573:         if hasattr(value, \"_meta\"):\n1574:             if self.to_field_name:\n1575:                 return value.serializable_value(self.to_field_name)\n1576:             else:\n1577:                 return value.pk\n1578:         return super().prepare_value(value)\n1579: \n1580:     def to_python(self, value):\n1581:         if value in self.empty_values:\n1582:             return None\n1583:         self.validate_no_null_characters(value)\n1584:         try:\n1585:             key = self.to_field_name or \"pk\"\n1586:             if isinstance(value, self.queryset.model):\n1587:                 value = getattr(value, key)\n1588:             value = self.queryset.get(**{key: value})\n1589:         except (\n1590:             ValueError,\n1591:             TypeError,\n1592:             self.queryset.model.DoesNotExist,\n1593:             ValidationError,\n1594:         ):\n1595:             raise ValidationError(\n1596:                 self.error_messages[\"invalid_choice\"],\n1597:                 code=\"invalid_choice\",\n1598:                 params={\"value\": value},\n1599:             )\n1600:         return value\n1601: \n1602:     def validate(self, value):\n1603:         return Field.validate(self, value)\n1604: \n1605:     def has_changed(self, initial, data):\n1606:         if self.disabled:\n1607:             return False\n1608:         initial_value = initial if initial is not None else \"\"\n1609:         data_value = data if data is not None else \"\"\n1610:         return str(self.prepare_value(initial_value)) != str(data_value)\n1611: \n1612: \n1613: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/forms/models.py%3FL1466-1612"
  }
}

{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"824: class Select(ChoiceWidget):\\n825:     input_type = \\\"select\\\"\\n826:     template_name = \\\"django/forms/widgets/select.html\\\"\\n827:     option_template_name = \\\"django/forms/widgets/select_option.html\\\"\\n828:     add_id_index = False\\n829:     checked_attribute = {\\\"selected\\\": True}\\n830:     option_inherits_attrs = False\\n831: \\n832:     def get_context(self, name, value, attrs):\\n833:         context = super().get_context(name, value, attrs)\\n834:         if self.allow_multiple_selected:\\n835:             context[\\\"widget\\\"][\\\"attrs\\\"][\\\"multiple\\\"] = True\\n836:         return context\\n837: \\n838:     @staticmethod\\n839:     def _choice_has_empty_value(choice):\\n840:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Return True if the choice's value is empty string or None.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n841:         value, _ = choice\\n842:         return value is None or value == \\\"\\\"\\n843: \\n844:     def use_required_attribute(self, initial):\\n845:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n846:         Don't render 'required' if the first <option> has a value, as that's\\n847:         invalid HTML.\\n848:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n849:         use_required_attribute = super().use_required_attribute(initial)\\n850:         # 'required' is always okay for <select multiple>.\\n851:         if self.allow_multiple_selected:\\n852:             return use_required_attribute\\n853: \\n854:         first_choice = next(iter(self.choices), None)\\n855:         return (\\n856:             use_required_attribute\\n857:             and first_choice is not None\\n858:             and self._choice_has_empty_value(first_choice)\\n859:         )\\n860: \\n861: \\n862: class NullBooleanSelect(Select):\\n863:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n864:     A Select Widget intended to be used with NullBooleanField.\\n865:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n866: \\n867:     def __init__(self, attrs=None):\\n868:         choices = (\\n869:             (\\\"unknown\\\", _(\\\"Unknown\\\")),\\n870:             (\\\"true\\\", _(\\\"Yes\\\")),\\n871:             (\\\"false\\\", _(\\\"No\\\")),\\n872:         )\\n873:         super().__init__(attrs, choices)\\n874: \\n875:     def format_value(self, value):\\n876:         try:\\n877:             return {\\n878:                 True: \\\"true\\\",\\n879:                 False: \\\"false\\\",\\n880:                 \\\"true\\\": \\\"true\\\",\\n881:                 \\\"false\\\": \\\"false\\\",\\n882:                 # For backwards compatibility with Django < 2.2.\\n899:             \\\"2\\\": True,\\n900:             \\\"3\\\": False,\\n901:         }.get(value)\\n902: \\n903: \\n904: class SelectMultiple(Select):\\n905:     allow_multiple_selected = True\\n906: \\n907:     def value_from_datadict(self, data, files, name):\\n908:         try:\\n909:             getter = data.getlist\\n910:         except AttributeError:\\n911: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/forms/widgets.py%3FL824-910\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "824: class Select(ChoiceWidget):\n825:     input_type = \"select\"\n826:     template_name = \"django/forms/widgets/select.html\"\n827:     option_template_name = \"django/forms/widgets/select_option.html\"\n828:     add_id_index = False\n829:     checked_attribute = {\"selected\": True}\n830:     option_inherits_attrs = False\n831: \n832:     def get_context(self, name, value, attrs):\n833:         context = super().get_context(name, value, attrs)\n834:         if self.allow_multiple_selected:\n835:             context[\"widget\"][\"attrs\"][\"multiple\"] = True\n836:         return context\n837: \n838:     @staticmethod\n839:     def _choice_has_empty_value(choice):\n840:         \"\"\"Return True if the choice's value is empty string or None.\"\"\"\n841:         value, _ = choice\n842:         return value is None or value == \"\"\n843: \n844:     def use_required_attribute(self, initial):\n845:         \"\"\"\n846:         Don't render 'required' if the first <option> has a value, as that's\n847:         invalid HTML.\n848:         \"\"\"\n849:         use_required_attribute = super().use_required_attribute(initial)\n850:         # 'required' is always okay for <select multiple>.\n851:         if self.allow_multiple_selected:\n852:             return use_required_attribute\n853: \n854:         first_choice = next(iter(self.choices), None)\n855:         return (\n856:             use_required_attribute\n857:             and first_choice is not None\n858:             and self._choice_has_empty_value(first_choice)\n859:         )\n860: \n861: \n862: class NullBooleanSelect(Select):\n863:     \"\"\"\n864:     A Select Widget intended to be used with NullBooleanField.\n865:     \"\"\"\n866: \n867:     def __init__(self, attrs=None):\n868:         choices = (\n869:             (\"unknown\", _(\"Unknown\")),\n870:             (\"true\", _(\"Yes\")),\n871:             (\"false\", _(\"No\")),\n872:         )\n873:         super().__init__(attrs, choices)\n874: \n875:     def format_value(self, value):\n876:         try:\n877:             return {\n878:                 True: \"true\",\n879:                 False: \"false\",\n880:                 \"true\": \"true\",\n881:                 \"false\": \"false\",\n882:                 # For backwards compatibility with Django < 2.2.\n883:                 \"2\": \"true\",\n884:                 \"3\": \"false\",\n885:             }[value]\n886:         except KeyError:\n887:             return \"unknown\"\n888: \n889:     def value_from_datadict(self, data, files, name):\n890:         value = data.get(name)\n891:         return {\n892:             True: True,\n893:             \"True\": True,\n894:             \"False\": False,\n895:             False: False,\n896:             \"true\": True,\n897:             \"false\": False,\n898:             # For backwards compatibility with Django < 2.2.\n899:             \"2\": True,\n900:             \"3\": False,\n901:         }.get(value)\n902: \n903: \n904: class SelectMultiple(Select):\n905:     allow_multiple_selected = True\n906: \n907:     def value_from_datadict(self, data, files, name):\n908:         try:\n909:             getter = data.getlist\n910:         except AttributeError:\n911: ",

subindex is not None:\n756:                     subindex += 1\n757:         return groups\n758: \n759:     def create_option(\n760:         self, name, value, label, selected, index, subindex=None, attrs=None\n761:     ):\n762:         index = str(index) if subindex is None else \"%s_%s\" % (index, subindex)\n763:         option_attrs = (\n764:             self.build_attrs(self.attrs, attrs) if self.option_inherits_attrs else {}\n765:         )\n766:         if selected:\n767:             option_attrs.update(self.checked_attribute)\n768:         if \"id\" in option_attrs:\n769:             option_attrs[\"id\"] = self.id_for_label(option_attrs[\"id\"], index)\n770:         return {\n771:             \"name\": name,\n772:             \"value\": value,\n773:             \"label\": label,\n774:             \"selected\": selected,\n775:             \"index\": index,\n776:             \"attrs\": option_attrs,\n777:             \"type\": self.input_type,\n778:             \"template_name\": self.option_template_name,\n779:             \"wrap_label\": True,\n780:         }\n781: \n782:     def get_context(self, name, value, attrs):\n783:         context = super().get_context(name, value, attrs)\n784:         context[\"widget\"][\"optgroups\"] = self.optgroups(\n785:             name, context[\"widget\"][\"value\"], attrs\n786:         )\n787:         return context\n788: \n789:     def id_for_label(self, id_, index=\"0\"):\n790:         \"\"\"\n791:         Use an incremented id for each option where the main widget\n792:         references the zero index.\n793:         \"\"\"\n794:         if id_ and self.add_id_index:\n795:             id_ = \"%s_%s\" % (id_, index)\n796:         return id_\n797: \n798:     def value_from_datadict(self, data, files, name):\n799:         getter = data.get\n800:         if self.allow_multiple_selected:\n801:             try:\n802:                 getter = data.getlist\n803:             except AttributeError:\n804:                 pass\n805:         return getter(name)\n806: \n807:     def format_value(self, value):\n808:         \"\"\"Return selected values as a list.\"\"\"\n809:         if value is None and self.allow_multiple_selected:\n810:             return []\n811:         if not isinstance(value, (tuple, list)):\n812:             value = [value]\n813:         return [str(v) if v is not None else \"\" for v in value]\n814: \n815:     @property\n816:     def choices(self):\n817:         return self._choices\n818: \n819:     @choices.setter\n820:     def choices(self, value):\n821:         self._choices = normalize_choices(value)\n822: \n823: ",

  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: import re\n2: \n3: from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError\n4: from django.forms.utils import RenderableFieldMixin, pretty_name\n5: from django.forms.widgets import MultiWidget, Textarea, TextInput\n6: from django.utils.functional import cached_property\n7: from django.utils.html import format_html, html_safe\n8: from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _\n9: \n10: __all__ = (\"BoundField\",)\n11: \n12: \n13: class BoundField(RenderableFieldMixin):\n14:     \"A Field plus data\"\n15: \n16:     def __init__(self, form, field, name):\n17:         self.form = form\n18:         self.field = field\n19:         self.name = name\n20:         self.html_name = form.add_prefix(name)\n21:         self.html_initial_name = form.add_initial_prefix(name)\n22:         self.html_initial_id = form.add_initial_prefix(self.auto_id)\n23:         if self.field.label is None:\n24:             self.label = pretty_name(name)\n25:         else:\n26:             self.label = self.field.label\n27:         self.help_text = field.help_text or \"\"\n28:         self.renderer = form.renderer\n29: \n30:     @cached_property\n31:     def subwidgets(self):\n32:         \"\"\"\n33:         Most widgets yield a single subwidget, but others like RadioSelect and\n34:         CheckboxSelectMultiple produce one subwidget for each choice.\n35: \n36:         This property is cached so that only one database query occurs when\n37:         rendering ModelChoiceFields.\n38:         \"\"\"\n39:         id_ = self.field.widget.attrs.get(\"id\") or self.auto_id\n40:         attrs = {\"id\": id_} if id_ else {}\n41:         attrs = self.build_widget_attrs(attrs)\n42:         return [\n43:             BoundWidget(self.field.widget, widget, self.form.renderer)\n44:             for widget in self.field.widget.subwidgets(\n45:                 self.html_name, self.value(), attrs=attrs\n46:             )\n47:         ]\n48: \n49:     def __bool__(self):\n50:         # BoundField evaluates to True even if it doesn't have subwidgets.\n51:         return True\n52: \n53:     def __iter__(self):\n54:         return iter(self.subwidgets)\n55: \n56:     def __len__(self):\n57:         return len(self.subwidgets)\n58: \n59:     def __getitem__(self, idx):\n60:         # Prevent unnecessary reevaluation when accessing BoundField's attrs\n61:         # from templates.\n62:         if not isinstance(idx, (int, slice)):\n63:             raise TypeError(\n64:                 \"BoundField indices must be integers or slices, not %s.\"\n65:                 % type(idx).__name__\n66:             )\n67:         return self.subwidgets[idx]\n68: \n69:     @property\n70:     def errors(self):\n71:         \"\"\"\n72:         Return an ErrorList (empty if there are no errors) for this field.\n73:         \"\"\"\n74:         return self.form.errors.get(\n75:             self.name, self.form.error_class(renderer=self.form.renderer)\n76:         )\n77: \n78:     @property\n79:     def template_name(self):\n80:         return self.field.template_name or self.form.renderer.field_template_name\n81: ",

            "content": "1361: \n1362: .. method:: BoundField.as_field_group()\n1363: \n1364:     Renders the field using :meth:`.BoundField.render` with default values\n1365:     which renders the ``BoundField``, including its label, help text and errors\n1366:     using the template's :attr:`~django.forms.Field.template_name` if set\n1367: ",
            "endLine": 1367,

            "endLine": 74,

          },
          {
            "content": "171: \n172: Field templates receive a context from :meth:`.BoundField.get_context`. By\n173: default, fields receive a dictionary with the following values:\n174: \n175: * ``field``: The :class:`~django.forms.BoundField`.\n176: \n177: ",
            "endLine": 177,

            "content": "1395: \n1396:         Note that no empty choice is created (regardless of the value of\n1397:         ``empty_label``) if a ``ModelChoiceField`` is required and has a\n1398:         default initial value, or a ``widget`` is set to\n1399:         :class:`~django.forms.RadioSelect` and the\n1400: ",

            "content": "32: password encoded in an older number of iterations and login request for a\n33: nonexistent user (which runs the default hasher's default number of iterations\n34: since Django 1.6).\n35: ",
            "endLine": 35,
            "startLine": 32

          {
            "content": "71:   :class:`~django.contrib.postgres.fields.ArrayField` serialization with\n72:   ``None`` values (:ticket:`26215`).\n73: \n74: ",

        "chunks": [

            "content": "393: * An unbound form has no data associated with it. When rendered to the user,\n394:   it will be empty or will contain default values.\n395: \n396: ",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"133:     @property\\n134:     def data(self):\\n135:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n136:         Return the data for this BoundField, or None if it wasn't given.\\n137:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n138:         return self.form._widget_data_value(self.field.widget, self.html_name)\\n139: \\n140:     def value(self):\\n141:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n142:         Return the value for this BoundField, using the initial value if\\n143:         the form is not bound or the data otherwise.\\n144:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n145:         data = self.initial\\n146:         if self.form.is_bound:\\n147:             data = self.field.bound_data(self.data, data)\\n148:         return self.field.prepare_value(data)\\n149: \\n150:     def _has_changed(self):\\n151:         field = self.field\\n152:         if field.show_hidden_initial:\\n153:             hidden_widget = field.hidden_widget()\\n154:             initial_value = self.form._widget_data_value(\\n155:                 hidden_widget,\\n156:                 self.html_initial_name,\\n157:             )\\n158:             try:\\n159:                 initial_value = field.to_python(initial_value)\\n160:             except ValidationError:\\n161:                 # Always assume data has changed if validation fails.\\n162:                 return True\\n163:         else:\\n164:             initial_value = self.initial\\n165:         return field.has_changed(initial_value, self.data)\\n166: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/forms/boundfield.py%3FL133-165\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "133:     @property\n134:     def data(self):\n135:         \"\"\"\n136:         Return the data for this BoundField, or None if it wasn't given.\n137:         \"\"\"\n138:         return self.form._widget_data_value(self.field.widget, self.html_name)\n139: \n140:     def value(self):\n141:         \"\"\"\n142:         Return the value for this BoundField, using the initial value if\n143:         the form is not bound or the data otherwise.\n144:         \"\"\"\n145:         data = self.initial\n146:         if self.form.is_bound:\n147:             data = self.field.bound_data(self.data, data)\n148:         return self.field.prepare_value(data)\n149: \n150:     def _has_changed(self):\n151:         field = self.field\n152:         if field.show_hidden_initial:\n153:             hidden_widget = field.hidden_widget()\n154:             initial_value = self.form._widget_data_value(\n155:                 hidden_widget,\n156:                 self.html_initial_name,\n157:             )\n158:             try:\n159:                 initial_value = field.to_python(initial_value)\n160:             except ValidationError:\n161:                 # Always assume data has changed if validation fails.\n162:                 return True\n163:         else:\n164:             initial_value = self.initial\n165:         return field.has_changed(initial_value, self.data)\n166: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/forms/boundfield.py%3FL133-165"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"413: \\n414:     def get_initial_for_field(self, field, field_name):\\n415:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n416:         Return initial data for field on form. Use initial data from the form\\n417:         or the field, in that order. Evaluate callable values.\\n418:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n419:         value = self.initial.get(field_name, field.initial)\\n420:         if callable(value):\\n421:             value = value()\\n422:         # If this is an auto-generated default date, nix the microseconds\\n423:         # for standardized handling. See #22502.\\n424:         if (\\n425:             isinstance(value, (datetime.datetime, datetime.time))\\n426:             and not field.widget.supports_microseconds\\n427:         ):\\n428:             value = value.replace(microsecond=0)\\n429:         return value\\n430: \\n431: \\n432: class Form(BaseForm, metaclass=DeclarativeFieldsMetaclass):\\n433:     \\\"A collection of Fields, plus their associated data.\\\"\\n434: \\n435:     # This is a separate class from BaseForm in order to abstract the way\\n436: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/forms/forms.py%3FL413-435\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "413: \n414:     def get_initial_for_field(self, field, field_name):\n415:         \"\"\"\n416:         Return initial data for field on form. Use initial data from the form\n417:         or the field, in that order. Evaluate callable values.\n418:         \"\"\"\n419:         value = self.initial.get(field_name, field.initial)\n420:         if callable(value):\n421:             value = value()\n422:         # If this is an auto-generated default date, nix the microseconds\n423:         # for standardized handling. See #22502.\n424:         if (\n425:             isinstance(value, (datetime.datetime, datetime.time))\n426:             and not field.widget.supports_microseconds\n427:         ):\n428:             value = value.replace(microsecond=0)\n429:         return value\n430: \n431: \n432: class Form(BaseForm, metaclass=DeclarativeFieldsMetaclass):\n433:     \"A collection of Fields, plus their associated data.\"\n434: \n435:     # This is a separate class from BaseForm in order to abstract the way\n436: ",

protection middleware </ref/csrf>` has been split into\\n380:   two classes -- ``CsrfViewMiddleware`` checks incoming requests, and\\n381:   ``CsrfResponseMiddleware`` processes outgoing responses. The combined\\n382:   ``CsrfMiddleware`` class (which does both) remains for\\n383:   backwards-compatibility, but using the split classes is now recommended in\\n384:   order to allow fine-grained control of when and where the CSRF processing\\n385: \",\"endLine\":385,\"startLine\":379},{\"content\":\"285: re-populating the database. This results in an immense speedup for most types\\n286: of unit tests.             "content": "508:     def _check_form(self, obj):\n509:         \"\"\"Check that form subclasses BaseModelForm.\"\"\"\n510:         if not _issubclass(obj.form, BaseModelForm):\n511:             return must_inherit_from(\n512:                 parent=\"BaseModelForm\", option=\"form\", obj=obj, id=\"admin.E016\"\n513:             )\n514: ",

      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "426: \n427: * :class:`~django.test.Client` and :class:`~django.test.AsyncClient` now\n428:   provide asynchronous methods, using an ``a`` prefix:\n429: ",
            "endLine": 429,
            "startLine": 426
          },
          {
            "content": "69:       {{ form.name }}\n70:       <div class=\"row\">\n71:         <div class=\"col\">\n72:           {{ form.email.label_tag }}\n73: ",
            "endLine": 73,
            "startLine": 69
          },
          {
            "content": "103:       {{ form.name.as_field_group }}\n104:       <div class=\"row\">\n105:         <div class=\"col\">{{ form.email.as_field_group }}</div>\n106:         <div class=\"col\">{{ form.password.as_field_group }}</div>\n107:       </div>\n108: ",
            "endLine": 108,
            "startLine": 103
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/releases/5.0.txt",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c/-/blob/docs/releases/5.0.txt"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "554: \n555:         class BaseAuthorFormSet(BaseModelFormSet):\n556:             def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):\n557: ",
            "endLine": 557,
            "startLine": 554
          },
          {
            "content": "2107: \n2108:         class BaseAuthorFormSet(BaseModelFormSet):\n2109:             def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):\n2110: ",
            "endLine": 2110,

          },
          {
            "content": "2216: \n2217:     def test_modelformset_factory_field_class_overrides(self):\n2218:         author = Author.objects.create(pk=1, name=\"Charles Baudelaire\")\n2219: ",
            "endLine": 2219,
            "startLine": 2216
          }
        ],
        "file": "tests/model_formsets/tests.py",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c/-/blob/tests/model_formsets/tests.py"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "124: \n125: In Django 1.1, :class:`~django.forms.models.BaseModelFormSet` now calls\n126: ``ModelForm.save()``.\n127: ",
            "endLine": 127,
            "startLine": 124
          },
          {
            "content": "379: * The :doc:`CSRF protection middleware </ref/csrf>` has been split into\n380:   two classes -- ``CsrfViewMiddleware`` checks incoming requests, and\n381:   ``CsrfResponseMiddleware`` processes outgoing responses. The combined\n382:   ``CsrfMiddleware`` class (which does both) remains for\n383:   backwards-compatibility, but using the split classes is now recommended in\n384:   order to allow fine-grained control of when and where the CSRF processing\n385: ",
            "endLine": 385,
            "startLine": 379
          },
          {
            "content": "285: re-populating the database. This results in an immense speedup for most types\n286: of unit tests. See the documentation for :class:`TestCase` and\n287: :class:`TransactionTestCase` for a full description, and some important notes\n288: on database support.\n289: ",
            "endLine": 289,
            "startLine": 285
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/releases/1.1.txt",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c/-/blob/docs/releases/1.1.txt"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "694: \n695: If you are using a :class:`ModelFormSet<django.forms.models.BaseModelFormSet>`,\n696: model instances for deleted forms will be deleted when you call\n697: ",
            "endLine": 697,
            "startLine": 694
          },
          {
            "content": "892:     set it as a string template name in order to override that for a particular\n893:     formset class.\n894: \n895: ",
            "endLine": 895,
            "startLine": 892
          },
          {
            "content": "6: \n7: .. class:: BaseFormSet\n8: \n9: ",
            "endLine": 9,
            "startLine": 6
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/topics/forms/formsets.txt",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c/-/blob/docs/topics/forms/formsets.txt"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "58: \n59: There are subclasses to make creating messages with common levels easier. When\n60: using them you can omit the ``level`` argument because it is implied by the\n61: class name.\n62: \n63: .. class:: Debug(msg, hint=None, obj=None, id=None)\n64: .. class:: Info(msg, hint=None, obj=None, id=None)\n65: .. class:: Warning(msg, hint=None, obj=None, id=None)\n66: .. class:: Error(msg, hint=None, obj=None, id=None)\n67: .. class:: Critical(msg, hint=None, obj=None, id=None)\n68: \n69: ",
            "endLine": 69,
            "startLine": 58
          },
          {
            "content": "689: The following checks are performed on any\n690: :class:`~django.contrib.admin.ModelAdmin` (or subclass) that is registered\n691: with the admin site:\n692: ",
            "endLine": 692,
            "startLine": 689
          },
          {
            "content": "835: * **admin.E206**: The value of ``formset`` must inherit from\n836:   ``BaseModelFormSet``.\n837: \n838: ",
            "endLine": 838,
            "startLine": 835
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/ref/checks.txt",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c/-/blob/docs/ref/checks.txt"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1882: \n1883:         class MyAdminFormSet(BaseModelFormSet):\n1884:             pass\n1885: ",
            "endLine": 1885,
            "startLine": 1882
          },
          {
            "content": "2914:     See :ref:`customizing-adminsite` for an example of customizing the\n2915:     :class:`AdminSite` class.\n2916: \n2917: ",
            "endLine": 2917,
            "startLine": 2914
          },
          {
            "content": "3145: \n3146: Customizing the :class:`AdminSite` class\n3147: ----------------------------------------\n3148: ",
            "endLine": 3148,
            "startLine": 3145
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/ref/contrib/admin/index.txt",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c/-/blob/docs/ref/contrib/admin/index.txt"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1314: \n1315: * :class:`ModelFormSet<django.forms.models.BaseModelFormSet>`\\s no longer\n1316:   delete instances when ``save(commit=False)`` is called. See\n1317: ",
            "endLine": 1317,
            "startLine": 1314
          },
          {
            "content": "1281: This change may cause test failures if you use ``select_for_update()``\n1282: in a test class which is a subclass of\n1283: :class:`~django.test.TransactionTestCase` rather than\n1284: :class:`~django.test.TestCase`.\n1285: \n1286: Contrib middleware removed from default ``MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES``\n1287: --------------------------------------------------------------\n1288: ",
            "endLine": 1288,
            "startLine": 1281
          },
          {
            "content": "887: \n888: * The new :class:`~django.http.JsonResponse` subclass of\n889:   :class:`~django.http.HttpResponse` helps easily create JSON-encoded\n890:   responses.\n891: ",
            "endLine": 891,
            "startLine": 887
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/releases/1.7.txt",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c/-/blob/docs/releases/1.7.txt"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1562:     def test_valid_case(self):\n1563:         class RealModelFormSet(BaseModelFormSet):\n1564:             pass\n1565: ",
            "endLine": 1565,
            "startLine": 1562
          },
          {
            "content": "839: \n840:     def test_not_list_filter_class(self):\n841:         class TestModelAdmin(ModelAdmin):\n842:             list_filter = [\"RandomClass\"]\n843: \n844: ",
            "endLine": 844,
            "startLine": 839
          },

            "content": "1545: \n1546:     def test_inline_without_formset_class(self):\n1547:         class ValidationTestInlineWithoutFormsetClass(TabularInline):\n1548:             model = ValidationTestInlineModel\n1549:             formset = \"Not a FormSet Class\"\n1550: \n1551:         class TestModelAdminWithoutFormsetClass(ModelAdmin):\n1552:             inlines = [ValidationTestInlineWithoutFormsetClass]\n1553: \n1554: ",
            "endLine": 1554,

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"346: \\n347: class BaseModelForm(BaseForm, AltersData):\\n348:     def __init__(\\n349:         self,\\n350:         data=None,\\n351:         files=None,\\n352:         auto_id=\\\"id_%s\\\",\\n353:         prefix=None,\\n354:         initial=None,\\n355:         error_class=ErrorList,\\n356:         label_suffix=None,\\n357:         empty_permitted=False,\\n358:         instance=None,\\n359:         use_required_attribute=None,\\n360:         renderer=None,\\n361:     ):\\n362:         opts = self._meta\\n363:         if opts.model is None:\\n364:             raise ValueError(\\\"ModelForm has no model class specified.\\\")\\n365:         if instance is None:\\n366:             # if we didn't get an instance, instantiate a new one\\n367:             self.instance = opts.model()\\n368:             object_data = {}\\n369:         else:\\n370:             self.instance = instance\\n371:             object_data = model_to_dict(instance, opts.fields, opts.exclude)\\n372:         # if initial was provided, it should override the values from instance\\n373:         if initial is not None:\\n374:             object_data.update(initial)\\n375:         # self._validate_(unique|constraints) will be set to True by\\n376:         # BaseModelForm.clean(). It is False by default so overriding\\n377:         # self.clean() and failing to call super will stop\\n378:         # validate_(unique|constraints) from being called.\\n379:         self._validate_unique = False\\n380:         self._validate_constraints = False\\n381:         super().__init__(\\n382:             data,\\n383:             files,\\n384:             auto_id,\\n385:             prefix,\\n386:             object_data,\\n387:             error_class,\\n388:             label_suffix,\\n389:             empty_permitted,\\n390:             use_required_attribute=use_required_attribute,\\n391:             renderer=renderer,\\n392:         )\\n393:         for formfield in self.fields.values():\\n394:             apply_limit_choices_to_to_formfield(formfield)\\n395: \\n396:     def _get_validation_exclusions(self):\\n397:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n398:         For backwards-compatibility, exclude several types of fields from model\\n399:         validation. See tickets #12507, #12521, #12553.\\n400:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n401:         exclude = set()\\n402:         # Build up a list of fields that should be excluded from model field\\n403:         # validation and unique checks.\\n404:         for f in self.instance._meta.fields:\\n405:             field = f.name\\n406:             # Exclude fields that aren't on the form. The developer may be\\n407:             # adding these values to the model after form validation.\\n408:             if field not in self.fields:\\n409:                 exclude.add(f.name)\\n410: \\n411:             # Don't perform model validation on fields that were defined\\n412:             # manually on the form and excluded via the ModelForm's Meta\\n413:             # class. See #12901.\\n414:             elif self._meta.fields and field not in self._meta.fields:\\n415:                 exclude.add(f.name)\\n416:             elif self._meta.exclude and field in self._meta.exclude:\\n417:                 exclude.add(f.name)\\n418: \\n419:             # Exclude fields that failed form validation. There's no need for\\n420:             # the model fields to validate them as well.\\n421:             elif field in self._errors:\\n422:                 exclude.add(f.name)\\n423: \\n424:             # Exclude empty fields that are not required by the form, if the\\n425:             # underlying model field is required. This keeps the model field\\n426:             # from raising a required error. Note: don't exclude the field from\\n427:             # validation if the model field allows blanks. If it does, the\\n428:             # blank value may be included in a unique check, so cannot be\\n429:             # excluded from validation.\\n430:             else:\\n431: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/forms/models.py%3FL346-430\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "346: \n347: class BaseModelForm(BaseForm, AltersData):\n348:     def __init__(\n349:         self,\n350:         data=None,\n351:         files=None,\n352:         auto_id=\"id_%s\",\n353:         prefix=None,\n354:         initial=None,\n355:         error_class=ErrorList,\n356:         label_suffix=None,\n357:         empty_permitted=False,\n358:         instance=None,\n359:         use_required_attribute=None,\n360:         renderer=None,\n361:     ):\n362:         opts = self._meta\n363:         if opts.model is None:\n364:             raise ValueError(\"ModelForm has no model class specified.\")\n365:         if instance is None:\n366:             # if we didn't get an instance, instantiate a new one\n367:             self.instance = opts.model()\n368:             object_data = {}\n369:         else:\n370:             self.instance = instance\n371:             object_data = model_to_dict(instance, opts.fields, opts.exclude)\n372:         # if initial was provided, it should override the values from instance\n373:         if initial is not None:\n374:             object_data.update(initial)\n375:         # self._validate_(unique|constraints) will be set to True by\n376:         # BaseModelForm.clean(). It is False by default so overriding\n377:         # self.clean() and failing to call super will stop\n378:         # validate_(unique|constraints) from being called.\n379:         self._validate_unique = False\n380:         self._validate_constraints = False\n381:         super().__init__(\n382:             data,\n383:             files,\n384:             auto_id,\n385:             prefix,\n386:             object_data,\n387:             error_class,\n388:             label_suffix,\n389:             empty_permitted,\n390:             use_required_attribute=use_required_attribute,\n391:             renderer=renderer,\n392:         )\n393:         for formfield in self.fields.values():\n394:             apply_limit_choices_to_to_formfield(formfield)\n395: \n396:     def _get_validation_exclusions(self):\n397:         \"\"\"\n398:         For backwards-compatibility, exclude several types of fields from model\n399:         validation. See tickets #12507, #12521, #12553.\n400:         \"\"\"\n401:         exclude = set()\n402:         # Build up a list of fields that should be excluded from model field\n403:         # validation and unique checks.\n404:         for f in self.instance._meta.fields:\n405:             field = f.name\n406:             # Exclude fields that aren't on the form. The developer may be\n407:             # adding these values to the model after form validation.\n408:             if field not in self.fields:\n409:                 exclude.add(f.name)\n410: \n411:             # Don't perform model validation on fields that were defined\n412:             # manually on the form and excluded via the ModelForm's Meta\n413:             # class. See #12901.\n414:             elif self._meta.fields and field not in self._meta.fields:\n415:                 exclude.add(f.name)\n416:             elif self._meta.exclude and field in self._meta.exclude:\n417:                 exclude.add(f.name)\n418: \n419:             # Exclude fields that failed form validation. There's no need for\n420:             # the model fields to validate them as well.\n421:             elif field in self._errors:\n422:                 exclude.add(f.name)\n423: \n424:             # Exclude empty fields that are not required by the form, if the\n425:             # underlying model field is required. This keeps the model field\n426:             # from raising a required error. Note: don't exclude the field from\n427:             # validation if the model field allows blanks. If it does, the\n428:             # blank value may be included in a unique check, so cannot be\n429:             # excluded from validation.\n430:             else:\n431: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/forms/models.py%3FL346-430"
  }

  "content": "{\"content\":\"99: def model_to_dict(instance, fields=None, exclude=None):\\n100:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n101:     Return a dict containing the data in ``instance`` suitable for passing as\\n102:     a Form's ``initial`` keyword argument.\\n103: \\n104:     ``fields`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, return only the\\n105:     named.\\n106: \\n107:     ``exclude`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, exclude the\\n108:     named from the returned dict, even if they are listed in the ``fields``\\n109:     argument.\\n110:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n111:     opts = instance._meta\\n112:     data = {}\\n113:     for f in chain(opts.concrete_fields, opts.private_fields, opts.many_to_many):\\n114:         if not getattr(f, \\\"editable\\\", False):\\n115:             continue\\n116:         if fields is not None and f.name not in fields:\\n117:             continue\\n118:         if exclude and f.name in exclude:\\n119:             continue\\n120:         data[f.name] = f.value_from_object(instance)\\n121:     return data\\n122: \\n123: \\n124: def apply_limit_choices_to_to_formfield(formfield):\\n125:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Apply limit_choices_to to the formfield's queryset if needed.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n126:     from django.db.models import Exists, OuterRef, Q\\n127: \\n128:     if hasattr(formfield, \\\"queryset\\\") and hasattr(formfield, \\\"get_limit_choices_to\\\"):\\n129:         limit_choices_to = formfield.get_limit_choices_to()\\n130:         if limit_choices_to:\\n131:             complex_filter = limit_choices_to\\n132:             if not isinstance(complex_filter, Q):\\n133:                 complex_filter = Q(**limit_choices_to)\\n134:             complex_filter &= Q(pk=OuterRef(\\\"pk\\\"))\\n135:             # Use Exists() to avoid potential duplicates.\\n136:             formfield.queryset = formfield.queryset.filter(\\n137:                 Exists(formfield.queryset.model._base_manager.filter(complex_filter)),\\n138:             )\\n139: \\n140: \\n141: def fields_for_model(\\n142:     model,\\n143:     fields=None,\\n144:     exclude=None,\\n145:     widgets=None,\\n146:     formfield_callback=None,\\n147:     localized_fields=None,\\n148:     labels=None,\\n149:     help_texts=None,\\n150:     error_messages=None,\\n151:     field_classes=None,\\n152:     *,\\n153:     apply_limit_choices_to=True,\\n154:     form_declared_fields=None,\\n155: ):\\n156:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n157:     Return a dictionary containing form fields for the given model.\\n158: \\n159:     ``fields`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, return only the\\n160:     named fields.\\n161: \\n162:     ``exclude`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, exclude the\\n163:     named fields from the returned fields, even if they are listed in the\\n164:     ``fields`` argument.\\n165: \\n166:     ``widgets`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a widget.\\n167: \\n168:     ``formfield_callback`` is a callable that takes a model field and returns\\n169:     a form field.\\n170: \\n171:     ``localized_fields`` is a list of names of fields which should be\\n172:     localized.\\n173: \\n174:     ``labels`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a label.\\n175: \\n176:     ``help_texts`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a help text.\\n177: \\n178:     ``error_messages`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a\\n179:     dictionary of error messages.\\n180: \\n181:     ``field_classes`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a form\\n182:     field class.\\n183: \\n184:     ``apply_limit_choices_to`` is a boolean indicating if limit_choices_to\\n185:     should be applied to a field's queryset.\\n186: \\n187:     ``form_declared_fields`` is a dictionary of form fields created directly on\\n188:     a form.\\n189:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n190:     form_declared_fields = form_declared_fields or {}\\n191:     field_dict = {}\\n192:     ignored = []\\n193:     opts = model._meta\\n194:     # Avoid circular import\\n195:     from django.db.models import Field as ModelField\\n196: \\n197:     sortable_private_fields = [\\n198:         f for f in opts.private_fields if isinstance(f, ModelField)\\n199:     ]\\n200:     for f in sorted(\\n201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/forms/models.py%3FL99-200\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "99: def model_to_dict(instance, fields=None, exclude=None):\n100:     \"\"\"\n101:     Return a dict containing the data in ``instance`` suitable for passing as\n102:     a Form's ``initial`` keyword argument.\n103: \n104:     ``fields`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, return only the\n105:     named.\n106: \n107:     ``exclude`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, exclude the\n108:     named from the returned dict, even if they are listed in the ``fields``\n109:     argument.\n110:     \"\"\"\n111:     opts = instance._meta\n112:     data = {}\n113:     for f in chain(opts.concrete_fields, opts.private_fields, opts.many_to_many):\n114:         if not getattr(f, \"editable\", False):\n115:             continue\n116:         if fields is not None and f.name not in fields:\n117:             continue\n118:         if exclude and f.name in exclude:\n119:             continue\n120:         data[f.name] = f.value_from_object(instance)\n121:     return data\n122: \n123: \n124: def apply_limit_choices_to_to_formfield(formfield):\n125:     \"\"\"Apply limit_choices_to to the formfield's queryset if needed.\"\"\"\n126:     from django.db.models import Exists, OuterRef, Q\n127: \n128:     if hasattr(formfield, \"queryset\") and hasattr(formfield, \"get_limit_choices_to\"):\n129:         limit_choices_to = formfield.get_limit_choices_to()\n130:         if limit_choices_to:\n131:             complex_filter = limit_choices_to\n132:             if not isinstance(complex_filter, Q):\n133:                 complex_filter = Q(**limit_choices_to)\n134:             complex_filter &= Q(pk=OuterRef(\"pk\"))\n135:             # Use Exists() to avoid potential duplicates.\n136:             formfield.queryset = formfield.queryset.filter(\n137:                 Exists(formfield.queryset.model._base_manager.filter(complex_filter)),\n138:             )\n139: \n140: \n141: def fields_for_model(\n142:     model,\n143:     fields=None,\n144:     exclude=None,\n145:     widgets=None,\n146:     formfield_callback=None,\n147:     localized_fields=None,\n148:     labels=None,\n149:     help_texts=None,\n150:     error_messages=None,\n151:     field_classes=None,\n152:     *,\n153:     apply_limit_choices_to=True,\n154:     form_declared_fields=None,\n155: ):\n156:     \"\"\"\n157:     Return a dictionary containing form fields for the given model.\n158: \n159:     ``fields`` is an optional list of field names.             "content": "204: * :attr:`~django.db.models.Field.db_index`\n205: * ``rel``: Used for related fields (like :class:`ForeignKey`). For advanced\n206:   use only.\n207: ",
            "endLine": 207,
            "startLine": 204
          },
          {
            "content": "475: The :meth:`~Field.rel_db_type` method is called by fields such as\n476: ``ForeignKey`` and ``OneToOneField`` that point to another field to determine\n477: their database column data types. For example, if you have an\n478: ",
            "endLine": 478,
            "startLine": 475
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/howto/custom-model-fields.txt",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c/-/blob/docs/howto/custom-model-fields.txt"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "493:   <django.db.models.fields.related.RelatedManager.set()>` method to the related\n494:   managers created by ``ForeignKey``, ``GenericForeignKey``, and\n495:   ``ManyToManyField``.\n496: ",
            "endLine": 496,
            "startLine": 493
          },
          {
            "content": "824: To improve performance, the ``add()`` methods of the related managers created\n825: by ``ForeignKey`` and ``GenericForeignKey`` changed from a series of\n826: ``Model.save()`` calls to a single ``QuerySet.update()`` call. The change means\n827: ",
            "endLine": 827,
            "startLine": 824
          },
          {
            "content": "227:   :attr:`~django.db.models.Options.order_with_respect_to` with a\n228:   ``GenericForeignKey``.\n229: \n230: ",
            "endLine": 230,
            "startLine": 227
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/releases/1.9.txt",

        "type": "file",

      },

      },
      {
        "chunks": [

            "content": "627: * ``Field._get_val_from_obj()`` will be removed in favor of\n628:   ``Field.value_from_object()``.\n629: \n630: ",
            "endLine": 630,
            "startLine": 627
          },
          {
            "content": "1075:   ``BaseModelAdmin``, ``ChangeList``, ``BaseCommentNode``,\n1076:   ``GenericForeignKey``, ``Manager``, ``SingleRelatedObjectDescriptor`` and\n1077:   ``ReverseSingleRelatedObjectDescriptor``.\n1078: ",
            "endLine": 1078,
            "startLine": 1075
          },
          {
            "content": "572: \n573: * The ``on_delete`` argument for ``ForeignKey`` and ``OneToOneField``  will be\n574:   required.\n575: ",
            "endLine": 575,
            "startLine": 572
          }
        ],

    "content": "966: class ForeignKey(ForeignObject):\n967:     \"\"\"\n968:     Provide a many-to-one relation by adding a column to the local model\n969:     to hold the remote value.\n970: \n971:     By default ForeignKey will target the pk of the remote model but this\n972:     behavior can be changed by using the ``to_field`` argument.\n973:     \"\"\"\n974: \n975:     descriptor_class = ForeignKeyDeferredAttribute\n976:     # Field flags\n977:     many_to_many = False\n978:     many_to_one = True\n979:     one_to_many = False\n980:     one_to_one = False\n981: \n982:     rel_class = ManyToOneRel\n983: \n984:     empty_strings_allowed = False\n985:     default_error_messages = {\n986:         \"invalid\": _(\n987:             \"%(model)s instance with %(field)s %(value)r is not a valid choice.\"\n988:         )\n989:     }\n990:     description = _(\"Foreign Key (type determined by related field)\")\n991: \n992:     def __init__(\n993:         self,\n994:         to,\n995:         on_delete,\n996:         related_name=None,\n997:         related_query_name=None,\n998:         limit_choices_to=None,\n999:         parent_link=False,\n1000:         to_field=None,\n1001:         db_constraint=True,\n1002:         **kwargs,\n1003:     ):\n1004:         try:\n1005:             to._meta.model_name\n1006:         except AttributeError:\n1007:             if not isinstance(to, str):\n1008:                 raise TypeError(\n1009:                     \"%s(%r) is invalid. First parameter to ForeignKey must be \"\n1010:                     \"either a model, a model name, or the string %r\"\n1011:                     % (\n1012:                         self.__class__.__name__,\n1013:                         to,\n1014:                         RECURSIVE_RELATIONSHIP_CONSTANT,\n1015:                     )\n1016:                 )\n1017:         else:\n1018:             # For backwards compatibility purposes, we need to *try* and set\n1019:             # the to_field during FK construction. It won't be guaranteed to\n1020:             # be correct until contribute_to_class is called. Refs #12190.\n1021:             to_field = to_field or (to._meta.pk and to._meta.pk.name)\n1022:         if not callable(on_delete):\n1023:             raise TypeError(\"on_delete must be callable.\")\n1024: \n1025:         kwargs[\"rel\"] = self.rel_class(\n1026:             self,\n1027:             to,\n1028:             to_field,\n1029:             related_name=related_name,\n1030:             related_query_name=related_query_name,\n1031:             limit_choices_to=limit_choices_to,\n1032:             parent_link=parent_link,\n1033:             on_delete=on_delete,\n1034:         )\n1035:         kwargs.setdefault(\"db_index\", True)\n1036: \n1037:         super().__init__(\n1038:             to,\n1039:             on_delete,\n1040: [\n1055:             *super().check(**kwargs),\n1056:             *self._check_on_delete(databases),\n1057:             *self._check_unique(),\n1058:         ]\n1059: \n1060:     def _check_on_delete_db_support(self, on_delete, feature_flag, databases):\n1061:         for db in databases:\n1062:             if not router.allow_migrate_model(db, self.model):\n1063: feature_flag\n1067:             ):\n1068:                 continue\n1069:             no_db_option_name = on_delete.__name__.removeprefix(\"DB_\")\n1070:             yield checks.Error(\n1071: gettext_lazy as _\\n31: \\n32: __all__ = (\\n33:     \\\"ModelForm\\\",\\n34:     \\\"BaseModelForm\\\",\\n35:     \\\"model_to_dict\\\",\\n36:     \\\"fields_for_model\\\",\\n37: construct_instance(form, instance, fields=None, exclude=None):\\n51:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n52:     Construct and return a model instance from the bound ``form``'s\\n53:     ``cleaned_data``, but do not save the returned instance to the database.\\n54:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n55:     from django.db import models\\n56: \\n57:     opts = instance._meta\\n58: \\n59:     cleaned_data = form.cleaned_data\\n60:     file_field_list = []\\n61:     for f in opts.fields:\\n62:         if (\\n63:             not f.editable\\n64:             or isinstance(f, models.AutoField)\\n65:             or f.name not in cleaned_data\\n66:         ):\\n67:             continue\\n68:         if fields is not None and f.name not in fields:\\n69:             continue\\n70:         if exclude and f.name in exclude:\\n71:             continue\\n72:         # Leave defaults for fields that aren't in POST data, except for\\n73:         # checkbox inputs because they don't appear in POST data if not\\n74:         # checked.\\n75:         if (\\n76:             f.has_default()\\n77:             and form[f.name].field.widget.value_omitted_from_data(\\n78:                 form.data, form.files, form.add_prefix(f.name)\\n79:             )\\n80:             and cleaned_data.get(f.name) in form[f.name].field.empty_values\\n81:         ):\\n82:             continue\\n83:         # Defer saving file-type fields until after the other fields, so a\\n84:         # callable upload_to can use the values from other fields.\\n85:         if isinstance(f, models.FileField):\\n86:             file_field_list.append(f)\\n87:         else:\\n88:             f.save_form_data(instance, cleaned_data[f.name])\\n89: \\n90:     for f in file_field_list:\\n91:         f.save_form_data(instance, model_to_dict(instance, fields=None, exclude=None):\\n100:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n101:     Return a dict containing the data in ``instance`` suitable for passing as\\n102:     a Form's ``initial`` keyword argument.\\n103: \\n104:     ``fields`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, return only the\\n105:     named.\\n106: \\n107:     ``exclude`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, exclude the\\n108:     named from the returned dict, even if they are listed in the ``fields``\\n109:     argument.\\n110:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n111:     opts = instance._meta\\n112:     data = {}\\n113:     for f in chain(opts.concrete_fields, opts.private_fields, opts.many_to_many):\\n114:         if not getattr(f, \\\"editable\\\", False):\\n115:             continue\\n116:         if fields is not None and f.name not in fields:\\n117:             continue\\n118:         if exclude and f.name in exclude:\\n119:             continue\\n120:         data[f.name] = f.value_from_object(instance)\\n121:     return data\\n122: \\n123: \\n124: def apply_limit_choices_to_to_formfield(formfield):\\n125:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Apply limit_choices_to to the formfield's queryset if needed.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n126:     from django.db.models import Exists, OuterRef, Q\\n127: \\n128:     if hasattr(formfield, \\\"queryset\\\") and hasattr(formfield, \\\"get_limit_choices_to\\\"):\\n129:         limit_choices_to = formfield.get_limit_choices_to()\\n130:         if limit_choices_to:\\n131:             complex_filter = limit_choices_to\\n132:             if not isinstance(complex_filter, Q):\\n133:                 complex_filter = Q(**limit_choices_to)\\n134:             complex_filter &= Q(pk=OuterRef(\\\"pk\\\"))\\n135:             # Use Exists() to avoid potential duplicates.\\n136:             formfield.queryset = formfield.queryset.filter(\\n137:                 Exists(formfield.queryset.model._base_manager.filter(complex_filter)),\\n138:             )\\n139: \\n140: \\n141: def fields_for_model(\\n142:     model,\\n143:     fields=None,\\n144:     exclude=None,\\n145:     widgets=None,\\n146:     formfield_callback=None,\\n147:     localized_fields=None,\\n148:     labels=None,\\n149:     help_texts=None,\\n150:     error_messages=None,\\n151: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/forms/models.py%3FL1-150\"}",
