46. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/django/forms/models.py"
}
Output
"Error: File does not exist. Note: your current working directory is /workspace."
47. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/get_file.py << 'EOF'\nimport os\nimport sys\n\n# Simple way to fetch the file content\n# We'll just reconstruct it from the repository\n\n# Create the models.py file with the fix\nmodels_py_content = '''\"\"\"\nHelper functions for creating Form classes from Django models\nand database field objects.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom itertools import chain\n\nfrom django.core.exceptions import (\n    NON_FIELD_ERRORS,\n    FieldError,\n    ImproperlyConfigured,\n    ValidationError,\n)\nfrom django.core.validators import ProhibitNullCharactersValidator\nfrom django.db.models.utils import AltersData\nfrom django.forms.fields import ChoiceField, Field\nfrom django.forms.forms import BaseForm, DeclarativeFieldsMetaclass\nfrom django.forms.formsets import BaseFormSet, formset_factory\nfrom django.forms.utils import ErrorList\nfrom django.forms.widgets import (\n    HiddenInput,\n    MultipleHiddenInput,\n    RadioSelect,\n    SelectMultiple,\n)\nfrom django.utils.choices import BaseChoiceIterator\nfrom django.utils.hashable import make_hashable\nfrom django.utils.text import capfirst, get_text_list\nfrom django.utils.translation import gettext\nfrom django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _\n\n__all__ = (\n    \"ModelForm\",\n    \"BaseModelForm\",\n    \"model_to_dict\",\n    \"fields_for_model\",\n    \"ModelChoiceField\",\n    \"ModelMultipleChoiceField\",\n    \"ALL_FIELDS\",\n    \"BaseModelFormSet\",\n    \"modelformset_factory\",\n    \"BaseInlineFormSet\",\n    \"inlineformset_factory\",\n    \"modelform_factory\",\n)\n\nALL_FIELDS = \"__all__\"\n\n\ndef construct_instance(form, instance, fields=None, exclude=None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Construct and return a model instance from the bound ``form``'s\n    ``cleaned_data``, but do not save the returned instance to the database.\n    \"\"\"\n    from django.db import models\n\n    opts = instance._meta\n\n    cleaned_data = form.cleaned_data\n    file_field_list = []\n    for f in opts.fields:\n        if (\n            not f.editable\n            or isinstance(f, models.AutoField)\n            or f.name not in cleaned_data\n        ):\n            continue\n        if fields is not None and f.name not in fields:\n            continue\n        if exclude and f.name in exclude:\n            continue\n        # Leave defaults for fields that aren't in POST data, except for\n        # checkbox inputs because they don't appear in POST data if not\n        # checked.\n        if (\n            f.has_default()\n            and form[f.name].field.widget.value_omitted_from_data(\n                form.data, form.files, form.add_prefix(f.name)\n            )\n            and cleaned_data.get(f.name) in form[f.name].field.empty_values\n        ):\n            continue\n        # Defer saving file-type fields until after the other fields, so a\n        # callable upload_to can use the values from other fields.\n        if isinstance(f, models.FileField):\n            file_field_list.append(f)\n        else:\n            f.save_form_data(instance, cleaned_data[f.name])\n\n    for f in file_field_list:\n        f.save_form_data(instance, cleaned_data[f.name])\n\n    return instance\n\n\n# ModelForms #################################################################\n\n\ndef model_to_dict(instance, fields=None, exclude=None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Return a dict containing the data in ``instance`` suitable for passing as\n    a Form's ``initial`` keyword argument.\n\n    ``fields`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, return only the\n    named.\n\n    ``exclude`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, exclude the\n    named from the returned dict, even if they are listed in the ``fields``\n    argument.\n    \"\"\"\n    from django.db.models import ForeignKey\n    \n    opts = instance._meta\n    data = {}\n    for f in chain(opts.concrete_fields, opts.private_fields, opts.many_to_many):\n        if not getattr(f, \"editable\", False):\n            continue\n        if fields is not None and f.name not in fields:\n            continue\n        if exclude and f.name in exclude:\n            continue\n        \n        # Handle ForeignKey fields with custom to_field\n        if isinstance(f, ForeignKey):\n            # Get the related object\n            value = getattr(instance, f.name, None)\n            if value is not None:\n                # Get the to_field name (the field being referenced)\n                to_field_name = f.remote_field.field_name\n                # Get the value from the to_field of the related object\n                data[f.name] = getattr(value, to_field_name, None)\n            else:\n                data[f.name] = None\n        else:\n            data[f.name] = f.value_from_object(instance)\n    return data\n'''\nprint(\"Content ready\")\nEOF\npython /tmp/get_file.py"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "Content ready"
}

{

  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! I've successfully read the entire django/forms/models.py file from the sourcegraph repository. Here's my analysis:\n\n## File Statistics\n\n**Total Line Count: 1,717 lines**\n\n## Key Sections\n\n### 1. **Module Header & Imports (Lines 1-45)**\n   - Module docstring describing helper functions for creating Form classes from Django models\n   - Necessary imports from django.core, django.db, django.forms, and django.utils\n   - `__all__` tuple defining public API exports\n\n### 2. **Utility Functions (Lines 47-257)**\n   - **construct_instance()** (50-93): Constructs model instance from form's cleaned_data\n   - **model_to_dict()** (99-121): Converts model instance to dict for form initialization\n   - **apply_limit_choices_to_to_formfield()** (124-138): Applies limit_choices_to to formfield queryset\n   - **fields_for_model()** (141-257): Main function returning form fields dict for a model\n\n### 3. **ModelForm Implementation (Lines 260-586)**\n   - **ModelFormOptions** class (260-271): Stores Meta options\n   - **ModelFormMetaclass** class (274-344): Metaclass for creating ModelForm classes\n   - **BaseModelForm** class (347-583): Core ModelForm logic with methods:\n     - `__init__()`: Initialize with data, instance, etc.\n     - `clean()`, `_post_clean()`: Validation methods\n     - `validate_unique()`, `validate_constraints()`: Unique/constraint validation\n     - `_save_m2m()`: Save many-to-many relationships\n     - `save()`: Save form instance\n   - **ModelForm** class (585-586): Simple wrapper with metaclass\n\n### 4. **ModelForm Factory (Lines 589-675)**\n   - **modelform_factory()**: Dynamically creates ModelForm classes with specified options\n\n### 5. **ModelFormSet Implementation (Lines 678-1105)**\n   - **BaseModelFormSet** class (681-1037): Formset for editing querysets with methods:\n     - `__init__()`, `get_queryset()`: Queryset management\n     - `validate_unique()`: Unique constraint validation\n     - `save()`, `save_existing_objects()`, `save_new_objects()`: Saving logic\n     - `add_fields()`: Add hidden pk field\n   - **modelformset_factory()** (1039-1104): Factory function for creating modelformsets\n\n### 6. **InlineFormSet Implementation (Lines 1107-1369)**\n   - **BaseInlineFormSet** class (1110-1225): Formset for related child objects\n   - **_get_foreign_key()** (1227-1303): Helper to find ForeignKey to parent\n   - **inlineformset_factory()** (1306-1369): Factory function for creating inline formsets\n\n### 7. **Field Classes (Lines 1372-1717)**\n   - **InlineForeignKeyField** (1375-1416): Field for inline foreign key values\n   - **ModelChoiceIteratorValue** (1419-1433): Wrapper for choice values\n   - **ModelChoiceIterator** (1436-1464): Iterator for model choice fields\n   - **ModelChoiceField** (1467-1610): Choice field with queryset as choices\n   - **ModelMultipleChoiceField** (1613-1710): Multiple choice field variant\n   - **modelform_defines_fields()** (1713-1716): Helper function to check if form defines fields\n\n## Architecture Summary\n\nThis file provides the complete infrastructure for:\n- Converting Django models to forms automatically\n- Creating formsets and inline formsets for managing multiple model instances\n- Field types specifically designed for model relationships (ForeignKey, ManyToMany)\n- Validation at both form and model levels\n- Saving form data back to model instances",

    }

\\n96: # ModelForms #################################################################\\n97: \\n98: \\n99: def model_to_dict(instance, fields=None, exclude=None):\\n100:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n101:     Return a dict containing the data in ``instance`` suitable for passing as\\n102:     a Form's ``initial`` keyword argument.\\n103: \\n104:     ``fields`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, return only the\\n105:     named.\\n106: \\n107:     ``exclude`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, exclude the\\n108:     named from the returned dict, even if they are listed in the ``fields``\\n109:     argument.\\n110:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n111:     opts = instance._meta\\n112:     data = {}\\n113:     for f in chain(opts.concrete_fields, opts.private_fields, opts.many_to_many):\\n114:         if not getattr(f, \\\"editable\\\", False):\\n115:             continue\\n116:         if fields is not None and f.name not in fields:\\n117:             continue\\n118:         if exclude and f.name in exclude:\\n119:             continue\\n120:         data[f.name] = f.value_from_object(instance)\\n121:     return data\\n122: \\n123: \\n124: def apply_limit_choices_to_to_formfield(formfield):\\n125:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Apply limit_choices_to to the formfield's queryset if needed.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n126:     from django.db.models import Exists, OuterRef, Q\\n127: \\n128:     if hasattr(formfield, \\\"queryset\\\") and hasattr(formfield, \\\"get_limit_choices_to\\\"):\\n129:         limit_choices_to = formfield.get_limit_choices_to()\\n130:         if limit_choices_to:\\n131:             complex_filter = limit_choices_to\\n132:             if not isinstance(complex_filter, Q):\\n133:                 complex_filter = Q(**limit_choices_to)\\n134:             complex_filter &= Q(pk=OuterRef(\\\"pk\\\"))\\n135:             # Use Exists() to avoid potential duplicates.\\n136:             formfield.queryset = formfield.queryset.filter(\\n137:                 Exists(formfield.queryset.model._base_manager.filter(complex_filter)),\\n138:             )\\n139: \\n140: \\n141: def fields_for_model(\\n142:     model,\\n143:     fields=None,\\n144:     exclude=None,\\n145:     widgets=None,\\n146:     formfield_callback=None,\\n147:     localized_fields=None,\\n148:     labels=None,\\n149:     help_texts=None,\\n150:     error_messages=None,\\n151:     field_classes=None,\\n152:     *,\\n153:     apply_limit_choices_to=True,\\n154:     form_declared_fields=None,\\n155: ):\\n156:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n157:     Return a dictionary containing form fields for the given model.\\n158: \\n159:     ``fields`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, return only the\\n160:     named fields.\\n161: \\n162:     ``exclude`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, exclude the\\n163:     named fields from the returned fields, even if they are listed in the\\n164:     ``fields`` argument.\\n165: \\n166:     ``widgets`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a widget.\\n167: \\n168:     ``formfield_callback`` is a callable that takes a model field and returns\\n169:     a form field.\\n170: \\n171:     ``localized_fields`` is a list of names of fields which should be\\n172:     localized.\\n173: \\n174:     ``labels`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a label.\\n175: \\n176:     ``help_texts`` is a dictionary of model a field's queryset.\\n186: \\n187:     ``form_declared_fields`` is a dictionary of form fields created directly on\\n188:     a form.\\n189:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n190:     form_declared_fields = attrs):\\n276:         new_class = super().__new__(mcs, name, bases, attrs)\\n277: \\n278:         if bases == (BaseModelForm,):\\n279:             return new_class\\n280: \\n281:         opts = new_class._meta = ModelFormOptions(getattr(new_class, \\\"Meta\\\", None))\\n282: \\n283:         # We check if a string was passed to `fields` or `exclude`,\\n284:         # which is likely to be a mistake where the user typed ('foo') instead\\n285:         # of ('foo',)\\n286:         for opt in [\\\"fields\\\", \\\"exclude\\\", \\\"localized_fields\\\"]:\\n287:             value = opts.model:\\n301:             # If a model is defined, extract form fields from it.\\n302:             if opts.fields is None and opts.exclude is None:\\n303:                 raise k, v in fields.items() if not v}\\n332:             missing_fields = none_model_fields.difference(new_class.declared_fields)\\n333:             if missing_fields:\\n334:                 message = \\\"Unknown field(s) (%s) specified for %s\\\"\\n335:                 message %= (\\\", \\\".join(missing_fields), opts.model.__name__)\\n336:                 raise FieldError(message)\\n337:  new_class.base_fields = fields\\n343: \\n344:         return new_class\\n345: \\n346: \\n347: class BaseModelForm(BaseForm, AltersData):\\n348:     def __init__(\\n349:         self,\\n350:         data=None,\\n351:         files=None,\\n352:         auto_id=\\\"id_%s\\\",\\n353:         prefix=None,\\n354:         initial=None,\\n355:         error_class=ErrorList,\\n356:         label_suffix=None,\\n357:         empty_permitted=False,\\n358:         instance=None,\\n359:         use_required_attribute=None,\\n360:         renderer=None,\\n361:     ):\\n362:         opts = self._meta\\n363:         if opts.model is None:\\n364:             raise ValueError(\\\"ModelForm has no model class specified.\\\")\\n365:         if instance is None:\\n366:             # if we didn't get an instance, instantiate a new one\\n367:             self.instance = opts.model()\\n368:             object_data = {}\\n369:         else:\\n370:             self.instance = instance\\n371:             object_data = model_to_dict(instance, opts.fields, opts.exclude)\\n372:         # if initial was provided, it should override the values from instance\\n373:         if initial is not None:\\n374:             object_data.update(initial)\\n375:         # self._validate_(unique|constraints) will be set to True by\\n376:         # BaseModelForm.clean(). It is False by default so overriding\\n377:         # self.clean() and failing to call super will stop\\n378:         # validate_(unique|constraints) from being called.\\n379:         self._validate_unique = False\\n380:         self._validate_constraints = False\\n381:         super().__init__(\\n382:             data,\\n383:             files,\\n384:             auto_id,\\n385:             prefix,\\n386:             object_data,\\n387:             error_class,\\n388:             label_suffix,\\n389:             empty_permitted,\\n390:             use_required_attribute=use_required_attribute,\\n391:             renderer=renderer,\\n392:         )\\n393:         for formfield in self.fields.values():\\n394:             apply_limit_choices_to_to_formfield(formfield)\\n395: \\n396:     def _get_validation_exclusions(self):\\n397:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n398:         For backwards-compatibility, exclude several types of fields from model\\n399:         validation. See tickets #12507, #12521, #12553.\\n400:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n401:         exclude = set()\\n402:         # Build up a list of fields that should be excluded from model field\\n403:         # validation and unique checks.\\n404:         for f in self.instance._meta.fields:\\n405:             field = f.name\\n406:             # Exclude fields that aren't on the form. The developer may be\\n407:             # adding these values to the model after form validation.\\n408:             if field not in self.fields:\\n409:                 exclude.add(f.name)\\n410: \\n411:             # Don't perform model validation on fields that were defined\\n412:             # manually on the form and excluded via the ModelForm's Meta\\n413:             # class. See #12901.\\n414:             elif self._meta.fields and field not in self._meta.fields:\\n415:                 exclude.add(f.name)\\n416:             elif self._meta.exclude and field in self._meta.exclude:\\n417:                 exclude.add(f.name)\\n418: \\n419:             # Exclude fields that failed form validation. There's no need for\\n420:             # the model fields to validate them as well.\\n421:             elif field in self._errors:\\n422:                 exclude.add(f.name)\\n423: \\n424:             # Exclude empty fields that are not required by the form, if the\\n425:             # underlying model field is required. This keeps the model field\\n426:             # from raising a required error. Note: don't exclude the field from\\n427:             # validation if the model field allows blanks. If it does, the\\n428:             # blank value may be included in a unique check, so cannot be\\n429:             # excluded from validation.\\n430:             else:\\n431:                 form_field = self.fields[field]\\n432:                 field_value = self.cleaned_data.get(field)\\n433:                 if (\\n434:                     not f.blank\\n435:                     and not form_field.required\\n436:                     and field_value in form_field.empty_values\\n437:                 ):\\n438:                     exclude.add(f.name)\\n439:         return exclude\\n440: \\n441:     def clean(self):\\n442:         self._validate_unique = True\\n443:         self._validate_constraints = True\\n444:         return self.cleaned_data\\n445: \\n446:     def _update_errors(self, errors):\\n447:         # Override any validation error messages defined at the model level\\n448:         # with those defined at the form level.\\n449:         opts = self._meta\\n450: \\n451:         # Allow the model generated by construct_instance() to raise\\n452:         # ValidationError and have them handled in the same way as others.\\n453:         if hasattr(errors, \\\"error_dict\\\"):\\n454:             error_dict = errors.error_dict\\n455:         else:\\n456:             error_dict = {NON_FIELD_ERRORS: errors}\\n457: \\n458:         for field, messages in error_dict.items():\\n459:             if (\\n460:                 field == NON_FIELD_ERRORS\\n461:                 and opts.error_messages\\n462:                 and NON_FIELD_ERRORS in opts.error_messages\\n463:             ):\\n464:                 error_messages = opts.error_messages[NON_FIELD_ERRORS]\\n465:             elif field in self.fields:\\n466:                 error_messages = self.fields[field].error_messages\\n467:             else:\\n468:                 continue\\n469: \\n470:             for message in messages:\\n471:                 if (\\n472:                     isinstance(message, ValidationError)\\n473:                     and message.code in error_messages\\n474:                 ):\\n475:                     message.message = error_messages[message.code]\\n476: \\n477:         self.add_error(None, errors)\\n478: \\n479:     def _post_clean(self):\\n480:         opts = self._meta\\n481: \\n482:         exclude = self._get_validation_exclusions()\\n483: \\n484:         # Foreign Keys being used to represent inline relationships\\n485:         # are excluded from basic field value validation. This is for two\\n486:         # reasons: firstly, the value may not be supplied (#12507; the\\n487:         # case of providing new values to the admin); secondly the\\n488:         # object being referred to may not yet fully exist (#12749).\\n489:         # However, these fields *must* be included in uniqueness checks,\\n490:         # so this can't be part of _get_validation_exclusions().\\n491:         for name, field in self.fields.items():\\n492:             if isinstance(field, InlineForeignKeyField):\\n493:                 exclude.add(name)\\n494: \\n495:         try:\\n496:             self.instance = construct_instance(\\n497:                 self, self.instance, opts.fields, opts.exclude\\n498:             )\\n499:         except ValidationError as e:\\n500:             self._update_errors(e)\\n501: \\n502:         try:\\n503:             self.instance.full_clean(\\n504:                 exclude=exclude, validate_unique=False, validate_constraints=False\\n505:             )\\n506:         except ValidationError as e:\\n507:             self._update_errors(e)\\n508: \\n509:         # Validate uniqueness and constraints if needed.\\n510:         if self._validate_unique:\\n511:             self.validate_unique()\\n512:         if self._validate_constraints:\\n513:             self.validate_constraints()\\n514: \\n515:     def validate_unique(self):\\n516:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n517:         Call the instance's validate_unique() method and update the form's\\n518:         validation errors if any were raised.\\n519:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n520:         exclude = self._get_validation_exclusions()\\n521:         try:\\n522:             self.instance.validate_unique(exclude=exclude)\\n523:         except ValidationError as e:\\n524:             self._update_errors(e)\\n525: \\n526:     def validate_constraints(self):\\n527:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n528:         Call the instance's validate_constraints() method and update the form's\\n529:         validation errors if any were raised.\\n530:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n531:         exclude = self._get_validation_exclusions()\\n532:         try:\\n533:             self.instance.validate_constraints(exclude=exclude)\\n534:         except ValidationError as e:\\n535:             self._update_errors(e)\\n536: \\n537:     def _save_m2m(self):\\n538:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n539:         Save the many-to-many fields and generic relations for this form.\\n540:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n541:         cleaned_data = self.cleaned_data\\n542:         exclude = self._meta.exclude\\n543:         fields = self._meta.fields\\n544:         opts = self.instance._meta\\n545:         # Note that for historical reasons we want to include also\\n546:         # private_fields here. (GenericRelation was previously a fake\\n547:         # m2m field).\\n548:         for f in chain(opts.many_to_many, opts.private_fields):\\n549:             if not hasattr(f, \\\"save_form_data\\\"):\\n550:                 continue\\n551:             if fields and f.name not in fields:\\n552:                 continue\\n553:             if exclude and f.name in exclude:\\n554:                 continue\\n555:             if f.name in cleaned_data:\\n556:                 f.save_form_data(self.instance, cleaned_data[f.name])\\n557: \\n558:     def save(self, commit=True):\\n559:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n560:         Save this form's self.instance object if commit=True. Otherwise, add\\n561:         a save_m2m() method to the form which can be called after the instance\\n562:         is saved manually at a later time. Return the model instance.\\n563:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n564:         if self.errors:\\n565:             raise ValueError(\\n566:                 \\\"The %s could not be %s because the data didn't validate.\\\"\\n567:                 % (\\n568:                     self.instance._meta.object_name,\\n569:                     \\\"created\\\" if self.instance._state.adding else \\\"changed\\\",\\n570:                 )\\n571:             )\\n572:         if commit:\\n573:             # If committing, save the instance and the m2m data immediately.\\n574:             self.instance.save()\\n575:             self._save_m2m()\\n576:         else:\\n577:             # If not committing, add a method to the form to allow deferred\\n578:             # saving of m2m data.\\n579:             self.save_m2m = self._save_m2m\\n580:         return self.instance\\n581: \\n582:     save.alters_data = True\\n583: \\n584: \\n585: class ModelForm(BaseModelForm, metaclass=ModelFormMetaclass):\\n586:     pass\\n587: \\n588: \\n589: def modelform_factory(\\n590:     model,\\n591:     form=ModelForm,\\n592:     fields=None,\\n593:     exclude=None,\\n594:     formfield_callback=None,\\n595:     widgets=None,\\n596:     localized_fields=None,\\n597:     labels=None,\\n598:     help_texts=None,\\n599:     error_messages=None,\\n600:     field_classes=None,\\n601: ):\\n602:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n603:     Return a ModelForm containing form fields for the given model. You can\\n604:     optionally pass a `form` argument to use as a starting point for\\n605:     constructing the ModelForm.\\n606: \\n607:     ``fields`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, include only\\n608:     the named fields in the returned fields. If omitted or '__all__', use all\\n609:     fields.\\n610: \\n611:     ``exclude`` is an optional list of field names. If provided, exclude the\\n612:     named fields from the returned fields, even if they are listed in the\\n613:     ``fields`` argument.\\n614: \\n615:     ``widgets`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a widget.\\n616: \\n617:     ``localized_fields`` is a list of names of fields which should be\\n618:     localized.\\n619: \\n620:     ``formfield_callback`` is a callable that takes a model field and returns\\n621:     a form field.\\n622: \\n623:     ``labels`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a label.\\n624: \\n625:     ``help_texts`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a help text.\\n626: \\n627:     ``error_messages`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a\\n628:     dictionary of error messages.\\n629: \\n630:     ``field_classes`` is a dictionary of model field names mapped to a form\\n631:     field class.\\n632:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n633:     # Create the inner Meta class. FIXME: ideally, we should be able to\\n634:     # construct a ModelForm without creating and passing in a temporary\\n635:     # inner class.\\n636: \\n637:     # Build up a list of attributes that the Meta object will have.\\n638:     attrs = {\\\"model\\\": model}\\n639:     if fields is not None:\\n640:         attrs[\\\"fields\\\"] = fields\\n641:     if exclude is not None:\\n642:         attrs[\\\"exclude\\\"] = exclude\\n643:     if widgets is not None:\\n644:         attrs[\\\"widgets\\\"] = widgets\\n645:     if localized_fields is not None:\\n646:         attrs[\\\"localized_fields\\\"] = localized_fields\\n647:     if labels is not None:\\n648:         attrs[\\\"labels\\\"] = labels\\n649:     if help_texts is not None:\\n650:         attrs[\\\"help_texts\\\"] = help_texts\\n651:     if error_messages is not None:\\n652:         attrs[\\\"error_messages\\\"] = error_messages\\n653:     if field_classes is not None:\\n654:         attrs[\\\"field_classes\\\"] = field_classes\\n655: \\n656:     # If parent form class already has an inner Meta, the Meta we're\\n657:     # creating needs to inherit from the parent's inner meta.\\n658:     bases = (form.Meta,) if hasattr(form, \\\"Meta\\\") else ()\\n659:     Meta = type(\\\"Meta\\\", bases, attrs)\\n660:     if formfield_callback:\\n661:         Meta.formfield_callback = staticmethod(formfield_callback)\\n662:     # Give this new form class a reasonable name.\\n663:     class_name = model.__name__ + \\\"Form\\\"\\n664: \\n665:     # Class attributes for the new form class.\\n666:     form_class_attrs = {\\\"Meta\\\": Meta}\\n667: \\n668:     if getattr(Meta, \\\"fields\\\", None) is None and getattr(Meta, \\\"exclude\\\", None) is None:\\n669:         raise ImproperlyConfigured(\\n670:             \\\"Calling modelform_factory without defining 'fields' or \\\"\\n671:             \\\"'exclude' explicitly is prohibited.\\\"\\n672:         )\\n673: \\n674:     # Instantiate type(form) in order to use the same metaclass as form.\\n675:     return type(form)(class_name, (form,), form_class_attrs)\\n676: \\n677: \\n678: # ModelFormSets ##############################################################\\n679: \\n680: \\n681: class BaseModelFormSet(BaseFormSet, AltersData):\\n682:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n683:     A ``FormSet`` for editing a queryset and/or adding new objects to it.\\n684:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n685: \\n686:     model = None\\n687:     edit_only = False\\n688: \\n689:     # Set of fields that must be unique among forms of this set.\\n690:     unique_fields = set()\\n691: \\n692:     def __init__(\\n693:         self,\\n694:         data=None,\\n695:         files=None,\\n696:         auto_id=\\\"id_%s\\\",\\n697:         prefix=None,\\n698:         queryset=None,\\n699:         *,\\n700:         initial=None,\\n701:         **kwargs,\\n702:     ):\\n703:         self.queryset = queryset\\n704:         self.initial_extra = initial\\n705:         super().__init__(\\n706:             **{\\n707:                 \\\"data\\\": data,\\n708:                 \\\"files\\\": files,\\n709:                 \\\"auto_id\\\": auto_id,\\n710:                 \\\"prefix\\\": prefix,\\n711:                 **kwargs,\\n712:             }\\n713:         )\\n714: \\n715:     def initial_form_count(self):\\n716:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the number of forms that are required in this FormSet.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n717:         if not self.is_bound:\\n718:             return len(self.get_queryset())\\n719:         return super().initial_form_count()\\n720: \\n721:     def _existing_object(self, pk):\\n722:         if not hasattr(self, \\\"_object_dict\\\"):\\n723:             self._object_dict = {o.pk: o for o in self.get_queryset()}\\n724:         return self._object_dict.get(pk)\\n725: \\n726:     def _get_to_python(self, field):\\n727:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n728:         If the field is a related field, fetch the concrete field's (that\\n729:         is, the ultimate pointed-to field's) to_python.\\n730:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n731:         while field.remote_field is not None:\\n732:             field = field.remote_field.get_related_field()\\n733:         return field.to_python\\n734: \\n735:     def _construct_form(self, i, **kwargs):\\n736:         pk_required = i < self.initial_form_count()\\n737:         if pk_required:\\n738:             if self.is_bound:\\n739:                 pk_key = \\\"%s-%s\\\" % (self.add_prefix(i), self.model._meta.pk.name)\\n740:                 try:\\n741:                     pk = self.data[pk_key]\\n742:                 except KeyError:\\n743:                     # The primary key is missing. The user may have tampered\\n744:                     # with POST data.\\n745:                     pass\\n746:                 else:\\n747:                     to_python = self._get_to_python(self.model._meta.pk)\\n748:                     try:\\n749:                         pk = to_python(pk)\\n750:                     except ValidationError:\\n751:                         # The primary key exists but is an invalid value. The\\n752:                         # user may have tampered with POST data.\\n753:                         pass\\n754:                     else:\\n755:                         kwargs[\\\"instance\\\"] = self._existing_object(pk)\\n756:             else:\\n757:                 kwargs[\\\"instance\\\"] = self.get_queryset()[i]\\n758:         elif self.initial_extra:\\n759:             # Set initial values for extra forms\\n760:             try:\\n761:                 kwargs[\\\"initial\\\"] = self.initial_extra[i - self.initial_form_count()]\\n762:             except IndexError:\\n763:                 pass\\n764:         form = super()._construct_form(i, **kwargs)\\n765:         if pk_required:\\n766:             form.fields[self.model._meta.pk.name].required = True\\n767:         return form\\n768: \\n769:     def get_queryset(self):\\n770:         if not hasattr(self, \\\"_queryset\\\"):\\n771:             if self.queryset is not None:\\n772:                 qs = self.queryset\\n773:             else:\\n774:                 qs = self.model._default_manager.get_queryset()\\n775: \\n776:             # If the queryset isn't already ordered we need to add an\\n777:             # artificial ordering here to make sure that all formsets\\n778:             # constructed from this queryset have the same form order.\\n779:             if not qs.ordered:\\n780:                 qs = qs.order_by(self.model._meta.pk.name)\\n781: \\n782:             # Removed queryset limiting here. As per discussion re: #13023\\n783:             # on django-dev, max_num should not prevent existing\\n784:             # related objects/inlines from being displayed.\\n785:             self._queryset = qs\\n786:         return self._queryset\\n787: \\n788:     def save_new(self, form, commit=True):\\n789:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Save and return a new model instance for the given form.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n790:         return form.save(commit=commit)\\n791: \\n792:     def save_existing(self, form, obj, commit=True):\\n793:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Save and return an existing model instance for the given form.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n794:         return form.save(commit=commit)\\n795: \\n796:     def delete_existing(self, obj, commit=True):\\n797:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Deletes an existing model instance.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n798:         if commit:\\n799:             obj.delete()\\n800: \\n801:     def save(self, commit=True):\\n802:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n803:         Save model instances for every form, adding and changing instances\\n804:         as necessary, and return the list of instances.\\n805:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n806:         if not commit:\\n807:             self.saved_forms = []\\n808: \\n809:             def save_m2m():\\n810:                 for form in self.saved_forms:\\n811:                     form.save_m2m()\\n812: \\n813:             self.save_m2m = save_m2m\\n814:         if self.edit_only:\\n815:             return self.save_existing_objects(commit)\\n816:         else:\\n817:             return self.save_existing_objects(commit) + self.save_new_objects(commit)\\n818: \\n819:     save.alters_data = True\\n820: \\n821:     def clean(self):\\n822:         self.validate_unique()\\n823: \\n824:     def validate_unique(self):\\n825:         # Collect unique_checks and date_checks to run from all the forms.\\n826:         all_unique_checks = set()\\n827:         all_date_checks = set()\\n828:         forms_to_delete = self.deleted_forms\\n829:         valid_forms = [\\n830:             form\\n831:             for form in self.forms\\n832:             if form.is_valid() and form not in forms_to_delete\\n833:         ]\\n834:         for form in valid_forms:\\n835:             exclude = form._get_validation_exclusions()\\n836:             unique_checks, date_checks = form.instance._get_unique_checks(\\n837:                 exclude=exclude,\\n838:                 include_meta_constraints=True,\\n839:             )\\n840:             all_unique_checks.update(unique_checks)\\n841:             all_date_checks.update(date_checks)\\n842: \\n843:         errors = []\\n844:         # Do each of the unique checks (unique and unique_together)\\n845:         for uclass, unique_check in all_unique_checks:\\n846:             seen_data = set()\\n847:             for form in valid_forms:\\n848:                 # Get the data for the set of fields that must be unique among\\n849:                 # the forms.\\n850:                 row_data = (\\n851:                     field if field in self.unique_fields else form.cleaned_data[field]\\n852:                     for field in unique_check\\n853:                     if field in form.cleaned_data\\n854:                 )\\n855:                 # Reduce Model instances to their primary key values\\n856:                 row_data = tuple(\\n857:                     (\\n858:                         d._get_pk_val()\\n859:                         if hasattr(d, \\\"_get_pk_val\\\")\\n860:                         # Prevent \\\"unhashable type\\\" errors later on.\\n861:                         else make_hashable(d)\\n862:                     )\\n863:                     for d in row_data\\n864:                 )\\n865:                 if row_data and None not in row_data:\\n866:                     # if we've already seen it then we have a uniqueness\\n867:                     # failure\\n868:                     if row_data in seen_data:\\n869:                         # poke error messages into the right places and mark\\n870:                         # the form as invalid\\n871:                         errors.append(self.get_unique_error_message(unique_check))\\n872:                         form._errors[NON_FIELD_ERRORS] = self.error_class(\\n873:                             [self.get_form_error()],\\n874:                             renderer=self.renderer,\\n875:                         )\\n876:                         # Remove the data from the cleaned_data dict since it\\n877:                         # was invalid.\\n878:                         for field in unique_check:\\n879:                             if field in form.cleaned_data:\\n880:                                 del form.cleaned_data[field]\\n881:                     # mark the data as seen\\n882:                     seen_data.add(row_data)\\n883:         # iterate over each of the date checks now\\n884:         for date_check in all_date_checks:\\n885:             seen_data = set()\\n886:             uclass, lookup, field, unique_for = date_check\\n887:             for form in valid_forms:\\n888:                 # see if we have data for both fields\\n889:                 if (\\n890:                     form.cleaned_data\\n891:                     and form.cleaned_data[field] is not None\\n892:                     and form.cleaned_data[unique_for] is not None\\n893:                 ):\\n894:                     # if it's a date lookup we need to get the data for all the\\n895:                     # fields\\n896:                     if lookup == \\\"date\\\":\\n897:                         date = form.cleaned_data[unique_for]\\n898:                         date_data = (date.year, date.month, date.day)\\n899:                     # otherwise it's just the attribute on the date/datetime\\n900:                     # object\\n901:                     else:\\n902:                         date_data = (getattr(form.cleaned_data[unique_for], lookup),)\\n903:                     data = (form.cleaned_data[field], *date_data)\\n904:                     # if we've already seen it then we have a uniqueness\\n905:                     # failure\\n906:                     if data in seen_data:\\n907:                         # poke error messages into the right places and mark\\n908:                         # the form as invalid\\n909:                         errors.append(self.get_date_error_message(date_check))\\n910:                         form._errors[NON_FIELD_ERRORS] = self.error_class(\\n911:                             [self.get_form_error()],\\n912:                             renderer=self.renderer,\\n913:                         )\\n914:                         # Remove the data from the cleaned_data dict since it\\n915:                         # was invalid.\\n916:                         del form.cleaned_data[field]\\n917:                     # mark the data as seen\\n918:                     seen_data.add(data)\\n919: \\n920:         if errors:\\n921:             raise ValidationError(errors)\\n922: \\n923:     def get_unique_error_message(self, unique_check):\\n924:         if len(unique_check) == 1:\\n925:             return gettext(\\\"Please correct the duplicate data for %(field)s.\\\") % {\\n926:                 \\\"field\\\": unique_check[0],\\n927:             }\\n928:         else:\\n929:             return gettext(\\n930:                 \\\"Please correct the duplicate data for %(field)s, which must be unique.\\\"\\n931:             ) % {\\n932:                 \\\"field\\\": get_text_list(unique_check, _(\\\"and\\\")),\\n933:             }\\n934: \\n935:     def get_date_error_message(self, date_check):\\n936:         return gettext(\\n937:             \\\"Please correct the duplicate data for %(field_name)s \\\"\\n938:             \\\"which must be unique for the %(lookup)s in %(date_field)s.\\\"\\n939:         ) % {\\n940:             \\\"field_name\\\": date_check[2],\\n941:             \\\"date_field\\\": date_check[3],\\n942:             \\\"lookup\\\": str(date_check[1]),\\n943:         }\\n944: \\n945:     def get_form_error(self):\\n946:         return gettext(\\\"Please correct the duplicate values below.\\\")\\n947: \\n948:     def save_existing_objects(self, commit=True):\\n949:         self.changed_objects = []\\n950:         self.deleted_objects = []\\n951:         if not self.initial_forms:\\n952:             return []\\n953: \\n954:         saved_instances = []\\n955:         forms_to_delete = self.deleted_forms\\n956:         for form in self.initial_forms:\\n957:             obj = form.instance\\n958:             # If the pk is None, it means either:\\n959:             # 1. The object is an unexpected empty model, created by invalid\\n960:             #    POST data such as an object outside the formset's queryset.\\n961:             # 2. The object was already deleted from the database.\\n962:             if not obj._is_pk_set():\\n963:                 continue\\n964:             if form in forms_to_delete:\\n965:                 self.deleted_objects.append(obj)\\n966:                 self.delete_existing(obj, commit=commit)\\n967:             elif form.has_changed():\\n968:                 self.changed_objects.append((obj, form.changed_data))\\n969:                 saved_instances.append(self.save_existing(form, obj, commit=commit))\\n970:                 if not commit:\\n971:                     self.saved_forms.append(form)\\n972:         return saved_instances\\n973: \\n974:     def save_new_objects(self, commit=True):\\n975:         self.new_objects = []\\n976:         for form in self.extra_forms:\\n977:             if not form.has_changed():\\n978:                 continue\\n979:             # If someone has marked an add form for deletion, don't save the\\n980:             # object.\\n981:             if self.can_delete and self._should_delete_form(form):\\n982:                 continue\\n983:             self.new_objects.append(self.save_new(form, commit=commit))\\n984:             if not commit:\\n985:                 self.saved_forms.append(form)\\n986:         return self.new_objects\\n987: \\n988:     def add_fields(self, form, index):\\n989:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Add a hidden field for the object's primary key.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n990:         from django.db.models import AutoField, ForeignKey, OneToOneField\\n991: \\n992:         self._pk_field = pk = self.model._meta.pk\\n993:         # If a pk isn't editable, then it won't be on the form, so we need to\\n994:         # add it here so we can tell which object is which when we get the\\n995:         # data back. Generally, pk.editable should be false, but for some\\n996:         # reason, auto_created pk fields and AutoField's editable attribute is\\n997:         # True, so check for that as well.\\n998: \\n999:         def pk_is_not_editable(pk):\\n1000:             return (\\n1001:                 (not pk.editable)\\n1002:                 or (pk.auto_created or isinstance(pk, AutoField))\\n1003:                 or (\\n1004:                     pk.remote_field\\n1005:                     and pk.remote_field.parent_link\\n1006:                     and pk_is_not_editable(pk.remote_field.model._meta.pk)\\n1007:                 )\\n1008:             )\\n1009: \\n1010:         if pk_is_not_editable(pk) or pk.name not in form.fields:\\n1011:             if form.is_bound:\\n1012:                 # If we're adding the related instance, ignore its primary key\\n1013:                 # as it could be an auto-generated default which isn't actually\\n1014:                 # in the database.\\n1015:                 pk_value = None if form.instance._state.adding else form.instance.pk\\n1016:             else:\\n1017:                 try:\\n1018:                     if index is not None:\\n1019:                         pk_value = self.get_queryset()[index].pk\\n1020:                     else:\\n1021:                         pk_value = None\\n1022:                 except IndexError:\\n1023:                     pk_value = None\\n1024:             if isinstance(pk, (ForeignKey, OneToOneField)):\\n1025:                 qs = pk.remote_field.model._default_manager.get_queryset()\\n1026:             else:\\n1027:                 qs = self.model._default_manager.get_queryset()\\n1028:             qs = qs.using(form.instance._state.db)\\n1029:             if form._meta.widgets:\\n1030:                 widget = form._meta.widgets.get(self._pk_field.name, HiddenInput)\\n1031:             else:\\n1032:                 widget = HiddenInput\\n1033:             form.fields[self._pk_field.name] = ModelChoiceField(\\n1034:                 qs, initial=pk_value, required=False, widget=widget\\n1035:             )\\n1036:         super().add_fields(form, index)\\n1037: \\n1038: \\n1039: def modelformset_factory(\\n1040:     model,\\n1041:     form=ModelForm,\\n1042:     formfield_callback=None,\\n1043:     formset=BaseModelFormSet,\\n1044:     extra=1,\\n1045:     can_delete=False,\\n1046:     can_order=False,\\n1047:     max_num=None,\\n1048:     fields=None,\\n1049:     exclude=None,\\n1050:     widgets=None,\\n1051:     validate_max=False,\\n1052:     localized_fields=None,\\n1053:     labels=None,\\n1054:     help_texts=None,\\n1055:     error_messages=None,\\n1056:     min_num=None,\\n1057:     validate_min=False,\\n1058:     field_classes=None,\\n1059:     absolute_max=None,\\n1060:     can_delete_extra=True,\\n1061:     renderer=None,\\n1062:     edit_only=False,\\n1063: ):\\n1064:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Return a FormSet class for the given Django model class.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1065:     meta = getattr(form, \\\"Meta\\\", None)\\n1066:     if (\\n1067:         getattr(meta, \\\"fields\\\", fields) is None\\n1068:         and getattr(meta, \\\"exclude\\\", exclude) is None\\n1069:     ):\\n1070:         raise ImproperlyConfigured(\\n1071:             \\\"Calling modelformset_factory without defining 'fields' or \\\"\\n1072:             \\\"'exclude' explicitly is prohibited.\\\"\\n1073:         )\\n1074: \\n1075:     form = modelform_factory(\\n1076:         model,\\n1077:         form=form,\\n1078:         fields=fields,\\n1079:         exclude=exclude,\\n1080:         formfield_callback=formfield_callback,\\n1081:         widgets=widgets,\\n1082:         localized_fields=localized_fields,\\n1083:         labels=labels,\\n1084:         help_texts=help_texts,\\n1085:         error_messages=error_messages,\\n1086:         field_classes=field_classes,\\n1087:     )\\n1088:     FormSet = formset_factory(\\n1089:         form,\\n1090:         formset,\\n1091:         extra=extra,\\n1092:         min_num=min_num,\\n1093:         max_num=max_num,\\n1094:         can_order=can_order,\\n1095:         can_delete=can_delete,\\n1096:         validate_min=validate_min,\\n1097:         validate_max=validate_max,\\n1098:         absolute_max=absolute_max,\\n1099:         can_delete_extra=can_delete_extra,\\n1100:         renderer=renderer,\\n1101:     )\\n1102:     FormSet.model = model\\n1103:     FormSet.edit_only = edit_only\\n1104:     return FormSet\\n1105: \\n1106: \\n1107: # InlineFormSets #############################################################\\n1108: \\n1109: \\n1110: class BaseInlineFormSet(BaseModelFormSet):\\n1111:     \\\"\\\"\\\"A formset for child objects related to a parent.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1112: \\n1113:     def __init__(\\n1114:         self,\\n1115:         data=None,\\n1116:         files=None,\\n1117:         instance=None,\\n1118:         save_as_new=False,\\n1119:         prefix=None,\\n1120:         queryset=None,\\n1121:         **kwargs,\\n1122:     ):\\n1123:         if instance is None:\\n1124:             self.instance = self.fk.remote_field.model()\\n1125:         else:\\n1126:             self.instance = instance\\n1127:         self.save_as_new = save_as_new\\n1128:         if queryset is None:\\n1129:             queryset = self.model._default_manager\\n1130:         if self.instance._is_pk_set():\\n1131:             qs = queryset.filter(**{self.fk.name: self.instance})\\n1132:         else:\\n1133:             qs = queryset.none()\\n1134:         self.unique_fields = {self.fk.name}\\n1135:         super().__init__(data, files, prefix=prefix, queryset=qs, **kwargs)\\n1136: \\n1137:         # Add the inline foreign key field to form._meta.fields if it's defined\\n1138:         # to make sure validation isn't skipped on that field.\\n1139:         if self.form._meta.fields and self.fk.name not in self.form._meta.fields:\\n1140:             self.form._meta.fields = list(self.form._meta.fields)\\n1141:             self.form._meta.fields.append(self.fk.name)\\n1142: \\n1143:     def initial_form_count(self):\\n1144:         if self.save_as_new:\\n1145:             return 0\\n1146:         return super().initial_form_count()\\n1147: \\n1148:     def _construct_form(self, i, **kwargs):\\n1149:         form = super()._construct_form(i, **kwargs)\\n1150:         if self.save_as_new:\\n1151:             mutable = getattr(form.data, \\\"_mutable\\\", None)\\n1152:             # Allow modifying an immutable QueryDict.\\n1153:             if mutable is not None:\\n1154:                 form.data._mutable = True\\n1155:             # Remove the primary key from the form's data, we are only\\n1156:             # creating new instances\\n1157:             form.data[form.add_prefix(self._pk_field.name)] = None\\n1158:             # Remove the foreign key from the form's data\\n1159:             form.data[form.add_prefix(self.fk.name)] = None\\n1160:             if mutable is not None:\\n1161:                 form.data._mutable = mutable\\n1162: \\n1163:         # Set the fk value here so that the form can do its validation.\\n1164:         fk_value = self.instance.pk\\n1165:         if self.fk.remote_field.field_name != self.fk.remote_field.model._meta.pk.name:\\n1166:             fk_value = getattr(self.instance, self.fk.remote_field.field_name)\\n1167:             fk_value = getattr(fk_value, \\\"pk\\\", fk_value)\\n1168:         setattr(form.instance, self.fk.attname, fk_value)\\n1169:         return form\\n1170: \\n1171:     @classmethod\\n1172:     def get_default_prefix(cls):\\n1173:         return cls.fk.remote_field.get_accessor_name(model=cls.model).replace(\\\"+\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n1174: \\n1175:     def save_new(self, form, commit=True):\\n1176:         # Ensure the latest copy of the related instance is present on each\\n1177:         # form (it may have been saved after the formset was originally\\n1178:         # instantiated).\\n1179:         setattr(form.instance, self.fk.name, self.instance)\\n1180:         return super().save_new(form, commit=commit)\\n1181: \\n1182:     def add_fields(self, form, index):\\n1183:         super().add_fields(form, index)\\n1184:         if self._pk_field == self.fk:\\n1185:             name = self._pk_field.name\\n1186:             kwargs = {\\\"pk_field\\\": True}\\n1187:         else:\\n1188:             # The foreign key field might not be on the form, so we poke at the\\n1189:             # Model field to get the label, since we need that for error\\n1190:             # messages.\\n1191:             name = self.fk.name\\n1192:             kwargs = {\\n1193:                 \\\"label\\\": getattr(\\n1194:                     form.fields.get(name), \\\"label\\\", capfirst(self.fk.verbose_name)\\n1195:                 )\\n1196:             }\\n1197: \\n1198:         # The InlineForeignKeyField assumes that the foreign key relation is\\n1199:         # based on the parent model's pk. If this isn't the case, set to_field\\n1200:         # to correctly resolve the initial form value.\\n1201:         if self.fk.remote_field.field_name != self.fk.remote_field.model._meta.pk.name:\\n1202:             kwargs[\\\"to_field\\\"] = self.fk.remote_field.field_name\\n1203: \\n1204:         # If we're adding a new object, ignore a parent's auto-generated key\\n1205:         # as it will be regenerated on the save request.\\n1206:         if self.instance._state.adding:\\n1207:             if kwargs.get(\\\"to_field\\\") is not None:\\n1208:                 to_field = self.instance._meta.get_field(kwargs[\\\"to_field\\\"])\\n1209:             else:\\n1210:                 to_field = self.instance._meta.pk\\n1211: \\n1212:             if to_field.has_default() and (\\n1213:                 # Don't ignore a parent's auto-generated key if it's not the\\n1214:                 # parent model's pk and form data is provided.\\n1215:                 to_field.attname == self.fk.remote_field.model._meta.pk.name\\n1216:                 or not form.data\\n1217:             ):\\n1218:                 setattr(self.instance, to_field.attname, None)\\n1219: \\n1220:         form.fields[name] = InlineForeignKeyField(self.instance, **kwargs)\\n1221: \\n1222:     def get_unique_error_message(self, unique_check):\\n1223:         unique_check = [field for field in unique_check if field != self.fk.name]\\n1224:         return super().get_unique_error_message(unique_check)\\n1225: \\n1226: \\n1227: def _get_foreign_key(parent_model, model, fk_name=None, can_fail=False):\\n1228:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1229:     Find and return the ForeignKey from model to parent if there is one\\n1230:     (return None if can_fail is True and no such field exists). If fk_name is\\n1231:     provided, assume it is the name of the ForeignKey field. Unless can_fail is\\n1232:     True, raise an exception if there isn't a ForeignKey from model to\\n1233:     parent_model.\\n1234:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1235:     # avoid circular import\\n1236:     from django.db.models import ForeignKey\\n1237: \\n1238:     opts = model._meta\\n1239:     if fk_name:\\n1240:         fks_to_parent = [f for f in opts.fields if f.name == fk_name]\\n1241:         if len(fks_to_parent) == 1:\\n1242:             fk = fks_to_parent[0]\\n1243:             all_parents = (*parent_model._meta.all_parents, parent_model)\\n1244:             if (\\n1245:                 not isinstance(fk, ForeignKey)\\n1246:                 or (\\n1247:                     # ForeignKey to proxy models.\\n1248:                     fk.remote_field.model._meta.proxy\\n1249:                     and fk.remote_field.model._meta.proxy_for_model not in all_parents\\n1250:                 )\\n1251:                 or (\\n1252:                     # ForeignKey to concrete models.\\n1253:                     not fk.remote_field.model._meta.proxy\\n1254:                     and fk.remote_field.model != parent_model\\n1255:                     and fk.remote_field.model not in all_parents\\n1256:                 )\\n1257:             ):\\n1258:                 raise ValueError(\\n1259:                     \\\"fk_name '%s' is not a ForeignKey to '%s'.\\\"\\n1260:                     % (fk_name, parent_model._meta.label)\\n1261:                 )\\n1262:         elif not fks_to_parent:\\n1263:             raise ValueError(\\n1264:                 \\\"'%s' has no field named '%s'.\\\" % (model._meta.label, fk_name)\\n1265:             )\\n1266:     else:\\n1267:         # Try to discover what the ForeignKey from model to parent_model is\\n1268:         all_parents = (*parent_model._meta.all_parents, parent_model)\\n1269:         fks_to_parent = [\\n1270:             f\\n1271:             for f in opts.fields\\n1272:             if isinstance(f, ForeignKey)\\n1273:             and (\\n1274:                 f.remote_field.model == parent_model\\n1275:                 or f.remote_field.model in all_parents\\n1276:                 or (\\n1277:                     f.remote_field.model._meta.proxy\\n1278:                     and f.remote_field.model._meta.proxy_for_model in all_parents\\n1279:                 )\\n1280:             )\\n1281:         ]\\n1282:         if len(fks_to_parent) == 1:\\n1283:             fk = fks_to_parent[0]\\n1284:         elif not fks_to_parent:\\n1285:             if can_fail:\\n1286:                 return\\n1287:             raise ValueError(\\n1288:                 \\\"'%s' has no ForeignKey to '%s'.\\\"\\n1289:                 % (\\n1290:                     model._meta.label,\\n1291:                     parent_model._meta.label,\\n1292:                 )\\n1293:             )\\n1294:         else:\\n1295:             raise ValueError(\\n1296:                 \\\"'%s' has more than one ForeignKey to '%s'. You must specify \\\"\\n1297:                 \\\"a 'fk_name' attribute.\\\"\\n1298:                 % (\\n1299:                     model._meta.label,\\n1300:                     parent_model._meta.label,\\n1301:                 )\\n1302:             )\\n1303:     return fk\\n1304: \\n1305: \\n1306: def inlineformset_factory(\\n1307:     parent_model,\\n1308:     model,\\n1309:     form=ModelForm,\\n1310:     formset=BaseInlineFormSet,\\n1311:     fk_name=None,\\n1312:     fields=None,\\n1313:     exclude=None,\\n1314:     extra=3,\\n1315:     can_order=False,\\n1316:     can_delete=True,\\n1317:     max_num=None,\\n1318:     formfield_callback=None,\\n1319:     widgets=None,\\n1320:     validate_max=False,\\n1321:     localized_fields=None,\\n1322:     labels=None,\\n1323:     help_texts=None,\\n1324:     error_messages=None,\\n1325:     min_num=None,\\n1326:     validate_min=False,\\n1327:     field_classes=None,\\n1328:     absolute_max=None,\\n1329:     can_delete_extra=True,\\n1330:     renderer=None,\\n1331:     edit_only=False,\\n1332: ):\\n1333:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1334:     Return an ``InlineFormSet`` for the given kwargs.\\n1335: \\n1336:     ``fk_name`` must be provided if ``model`` has more than one ``ForeignKey``\\n1337:     to ``parent_model``.\\n1338:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1339:     fk = _get_foreign_key(parent_model, model, fk_name=fk_name)\\n1340:     # enforce a max_num=1 when the foreign key to the parent model is unique.\\n1341:     if fk.unique:\\n1342:         max_num = 1\\n1343:     kwargs = {\\n1344:         \\\"form\\\": form,\\n1345:         \\\"formfield_callback\\\": formfield_callback,\\n1346:         \\\"formset\\\": formset,\\n1347:         \\\"extra\\\": extra,\\n1348:         \\\"can_delete\\\": can_delete,\\n1349:         \\\"can_order\\\": can_order,\\n1350:         \\\"fields\\\": fields,\\n1351:         \\\"exclude\\\": exclude,\\n1352:         \\\"min_num\\\": min_num,\\n1353:         \\\"max_num\\\": max_num,\\n1354:         \\\"widgets\\\": widgets,\\n1355:         \\\"validate_min\\\": validate_min,\\n1356:         \\\"validate_max\\\": validate_max,\\n1357:         \\\"localized_fields\\\": localized_fields,\\n1358:         \\\"labels\\\": labels,\\n1359:         \\\"help_texts\\\": help_texts,\\n1360:         \\\"error_messages\\\": error_messages,\\n1361:         \\\"field_classes\\\": field_classes,\\n1362:         \\\"absolute_max\\\": absolute_max,\\n1363:         \\\"can_delete_extra\\\": can_delete_extra,\\n1364:         \\\"renderer\\\": renderer,\\n1365:         \\\"edit_only\\\": edit_only,\\n1366:     }\\n1367:     FormSet = modelformset_factory(model, **kwargs)\\n1368:     FormSet.fk = fk\\n1369:     return FormSet\\n1370: \\n1371: \\n1372: # Fields #####################################################################\\n1373: \\n1374: \\n1375: class InlineForeignKeyField(Field):\\n1376:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1377:     A basic integer field that deals with validating the given value to a\\n1378:     given parent instance in an inline.\\n1379:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1380: \\n1381:     widget = HiddenInput\\n1382:     default_error_messages = {\\n1383:         \\\"invalid_choice\\\": _(\\\"The inline value did not match the parent instance.\\\"),\\n1384:     }\\n1385: \\n1386:     def __init__(self, parent_instance, *args, pk_field=False, to_field=None, **kwargs):\\n1387:         self.parent_instance = parent_instance\\n1388:         self.pk_field = pk_field\\n1389:         self.to_field = to_field\\n1390:         if self.parent_instance is not None:\\n1391:             if self.to_field:\\n1392:                 kwargs[\\\"initial\\\"] = getattr(self.parent_instance, self.to_field)\\n1393:             else:\\n1394:                 kwargs[\\\"initial\\\"] = self.parent_instance.pk\\n1395:         kwargs[\\\"required\\\"] = False\\n1396:         super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)\\n1397: \\n1398:     def clean(self, value):\\n1399:         if value in self.empty_values:\\n1400:             if self.pk_field:\\n1401:                 return None\\n1402:             # if there is no value act as we did before.\\n1403:             return self.parent_instance\\n1404:         # ensure the we compare the values as equal types.\\n1405:         if self.to_field:\\n1406:             orig = getattr(self.parent_instance, self.to_field)\\n1407:         else:\\n1408:             orig = self.parent_instance.pk\\n1409:         if str(value) != str(orig):\\n1410:             raise ValidationError(\\n1411:                 self.error_messages[\\\"invalid_choice\\\"], code=\\\"invalid_choice\\\"\\n1412:             )\\n1413:         return self.parent_instance\\n1414: \\n1415:     def has_changed(self, initial, data):\\n1416:         return False\\n1417: \\n1418: \\n1419: class ModelChoiceIteratorValue:\\n1420:     def __init__(self, value, instance):\\n1421:         self.value = value\\n1422:         self.instance = instance\\n1423: \\n1424:     def __str__(self):\\n1425:         return str(self.value)\\n1426: \\n1427:     def __hash__(self):\\n1428:         return hash(self.value)\\n1429: \\n1430:     def __eq__(self, other):\\n1431:         if isinstance(other, ModelChoiceIteratorValue):\\n1432:             other = other.value\\n1433:         return self.value == other\\n1434: \\n1435: \\n1436: class ModelChoiceIterator(BaseChoiceIterator):\\n1437:     def __init__(self, field):\\n1438:         self.field = field\\n1439:         self.queryset = field.queryset\\n1440: \\n1441:     def __iter__(self):\\n1442:         if self.field.empty_label is not None:\\n1443:             yield (\\\"\\\", self.field.empty_label)\\n1444:         queryset = self.queryset\\n1445:         # Can't use iterator() when queryset uses prefetch_related()\\n1446:         if not queryset._prefetch_related_lookups:\\n1447:             queryset = queryset.iterator()\\n1448:         for obj in queryset:\\n1449:             yield self.choice(obj)\\n1450: \\n1451:     def __len__(self):\\n1452:         # count() adds a query but uses less memory since the QuerySet results\\n1453:         # won't be cached. In most cases, the choices will only be iterated on,\\n1454:         # and __len__() won't be called.\\n1455:         return self.queryset.count() + (1 if self.field.empty_label is not None else 0)\\n1456: \\n1457:     def __bool__(self):\\n1458:         return self.field.empty_label is not None or self.queryset.exists()\\n1459: \\n1460:     def choice(self, obj):\\n1461:         return (\\n1462:             ModelChoiceIteratorValue(self.field.prepare_value(obj), obj),\\n1463:             self.field.label_from_instance(obj),\\n1464:         )\\n1465: \\n1466: \\n1467: class ModelChoiceField(ChoiceField):\\n1468:     \\\"\\\"\\\"A ChoiceField whose choices are a model QuerySet.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1469: \\n1470:     # This class is a subclass of ChoiceField for purity, but it doesn't\\n1471:     # actually use any of ChoiceField's implementation.\\n1472:     default_error_messages = {\\n1473:         \\\"invalid_choice\\\": _(\\n1474:             \\\"Select a valid choice. That choice is not one of the available choices.\\\"\\n1475:         ),\\n1476:     }\\n1477:     iterator = ModelChoiceIterator\\n1478: \\n1479:     def __init__(\\n1480:         self,\\n1481:         queryset,\\n1482:         *,\\n1483:         empty_label=\\\"---------\\\",\\n1484:         required=True,\\n1485:         widget=None,\\n1486:         label=None,\\n1487:         initial=None,\\n1488:         help_text=\\\"\\\",\\n1489:         to_field_name=None,\\n1490:         limit_choices_to=None,\\n1491:         blank=False,\\n1492:         **kwargs,\\n1493:     ):\\n1494:         # Call Field instead of ChoiceField __init__() because we don't need\\n1495:         # ChoiceField.__init__().\\n1496:         Field.__init__(\\n1497:             self,\\n1498:             required=required,\\n1499:             widget=widget,\\n1500:             label=label,\\n1501:             initial=initial,\\n1502:             help_text=help_text,\\n1503:             **kwargs,\\n1504:         )\\n1505:         if (required and initial is not None) or (\\n1506:             isinstance(self.widget, RadioSelect) and not blank\\n1507:         ):\\n1508:             self.empty_label = None\\n1509:         else:\\n1510:             self.empty_label = empty_label\\n1511:         self.queryset = queryset\\n1512:         self.limit_choices_to = limit_choices_to  # limit the queryset later.\\n1513:         self.to_field_name = to_field_name\\n1514: \\n1515:     def validate_no_null_characters(self, value):\\n1516:         non_null_character_validator = ProhibitNullCharactersValidator()\\n1517:         return non_null_character_validator(value)\\n1518: \\n1519:     def get_limit_choices_to(self):\\n1520:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1521:         Return ``limit_choices_to`` for this form field.\\n1522: \\n1523:         If it is a callable, invoke it and return the result.\\n1524:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1525:         if callable(self.limit_choices_to):\\n1526:             return self.limit_choices_to()\\n1527:         return self.limit_choices_to\\n1528: \\n1529:     def __deepcopy__(self, memo):\\n1530:         result = super(ChoiceField, self).__deepcopy__(memo)\\n1531:         # Need to force a new ModelChoiceIterator to be created, bug #11183\\n1532:         if self.queryset is not None:\\n1533:             result.queryset = self.queryset.all()\\n1534:         return result\\n1535: \\n1536:     def _get_queryset(self):\\n1537:         return self._queryset\\n1538: \\n1539:     def _set_queryset(self, queryset):\\n1540:         self._queryset = None if queryset is None else queryset.all()\\n1541:         self.widget.choices = self.choices\\n1542: \\n1543:     queryset = property(_get_queryset, _set_queryset)\\n1544: \\n1545:     # this method will be used to create object labels by the QuerySetIterator.\\n1546:     # Override it to customize the label.\\n1547:     def label_from_instance(self, obj):\\n1548:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1549:         Convert objects into strings and generate the labels for the choices\\n1550:         presented by this object. Subclasses can override this method to\\n1551:         customize the display of the choices.\\n1552:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1553:         return str(obj)\\n1554: \\n1555:     def _get_choices(self):\\n1556:         # If self._choices is set, then somebody must have manually set\\n1557:         # the property self.choices. In this case, just return self._choices.\\n1558:         if hasattr(self, \\\"_choices\\\"):\\n1559:             return self._choices\\n1560: \\n1561:         # Otherwise, execute the QuerySet in self.queryset to determine the\\n1562:         # choices dynamically. Return a fresh ModelChoiceIterator that has not\\n1563:         # been consumed. Note that we're instantiating a new\\n1564:         # ModelChoiceIterator *each* time _get_choices() is called (and, thus,\\n1565:         # each time self.choices is accessed) so that we can ensure the\\n1566:         # QuerySet has not been consumed. This construct might look complicated\\n1567:         # but it allows for lazy evaluation of the queryset.\\n1568:         return self.iterator(self)\\n1569: \\n1570:     choices = property(_get_choices, ChoiceField.choices.fset)\\n1571: \\n1572:     def prepare_value(self, value):\\n1573:         if hasattr(value, \\\"_meta\\\"):\\n1574:             if self.to_field_name:\\n1575:                 return value.serializable_value(self.to_field_name)\\n1576:             else:\\n1577:                 return value.pk\\n1578:         return super().prepare_value(value)\\n1579: \\n1580:     def to_python(self, value):\\n1581:         if value in self.empty_values:\\n1582:             return None\\n1583:         self.validate_no_null_characters(value)\\n1584:         try:\\n1585:             key = self.to_field_name or \\\"pk\\\"\\n1586:             if isinstance(value, self.queryset.model):\\n1587:                 value = getattr(value, key)\\n1588:             value = self.queryset.get(**{key: value})\\n1589:         except (\\n1590:             ValueError,\\n1591:             TypeError,\\n1592:             self.queryset.model.DoesNotExist,\\n1593:             ValidationError,\\n1594:         ):\\n1595:             raise ValidationError(\\n1596:                 self.error_messages[\\\"invalid_choice\\\"],\\n1597:                 code=\\\"invalid_choice\\\",\\n1598:                 params={\\\"value\\\": value},\\n1599:             )\\n1600:         return value\\n1601: \\n1602:     def validate(self, value):\\n1603:         return Field.validate(self, value)\\n1604: \\n1605:     def has_changed(self, initial, data):\\n1606:         if self.disabled:\\n1607:             return False\\n1608:         initial_value = initial if initial is not None else \\\"\\\"\\n1609:         data_value = data if data is not None else \\\"\\\"\\n1610:         return str(self.prepare_value(initial_value)) != str(data_value)\\n1611: \\n1612: \\n1613: class ModelMultipleChoiceField(ModelChoiceField):\\n1614:     \\\"\\\"\\\"A MultipleChoiceField whose choices are a model QuerySet.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1615: \\n1616:     widget = SelectMultiple\\n1617:     hidden_widget = MultipleHiddenInput\\n1618:     default_error_messages = {\\n1619:         \\\"invalid_list\\\": _(\\\"Enter a list of values.\\\"),\\n1620:         \\\"invalid_choice\\\": _(\\n1621:             \\\"Select a valid choice. %(value)s is not one of the available choices.\\\"\\n1622:         ),\\n1623:         \\\"invalid_pk_value\\\": _(\\\"\u201c%(pk)s\u201d is not a valid value.\\\"),\\n1624:     }\\n1625: \\n1626:     def __init__(self, queryset, **kwargs):\\n1627:         super().__init__(queryset, empty_label=None, **kwargs)\\n1628: \\n1629:     def to_python(self, value):\\n1630:         if not value:\\n1631:             return []\\n1632:         return list(self._check_values(value))\\n1633: \\n1634:     def clean(self, value):\\n1635:         value = self.prepare_value(value)\\n1636:         if self.required and not value:\\n1637:             raise ValidationError(self.error_messages[\\\"required\\\"], code=\\\"required\\\")\\n1638:         elif not self.required and not value:\\n1639:             return self.queryset.none()\\n1640:         if not isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):\\n1641:             raise ValidationError(\\n1642:                 self.error_messages[\\\"invalid_list\\\"],\\n1643:                 code=\\\"invalid_list\\\",\\n1644:             )\\n1645:         qs = self._check_values(value)\\n1646:         # Since this overrides the inherited ModelChoiceField.clean\\n1647:         # we run custom validators here\\n1648:         self.run_validators(value)\\n1649:         return qs\\n1650: \\n1651:     def _check_values(self, value):\\n1652:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1653:         Given a list of possible PK values, return a QuerySet of the\\n1654:         corresponding objects. Raise a ValidationError if a given value is\\n1655:         invalid (not a valid PK, not in the queryset, etc.)\\n1656:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1657:         key = self.to_field_name or \\\"pk\\\"\\n1658:         # deduplicate given values to avoid creating many querysets or\\n1659:         # requiring the database backend deduplicate efficiently.\\n1660:         try:\\n1661:             value = frozenset(value)\\n1662:         except TypeError:\\n1663:             # list of lists isn't hashable, for example\\n1664:             raise ValidationError(\\n1665:                 self.error_messages[\\\"invalid_list\\\"],\\n1666:                 code=\\\"invalid_list\\\",\\n1667:             )\\n1668:         for pk in value:\\n1669:             self.validate_no_null_characters(pk)\\n1670:             try:\\n1671:                 self.queryset.filter(**{key: pk})\\n1672:             except (ValueError, TypeError, ValidationError):\\n1673:                 raise ValidationError(\\n1674:                     self.error_messages[\\\"invalid_pk_value\\\"],\\n1675:                     code=\\\"invalid_pk_value\\\",\\n1676:                     params={\\\"pk\\\": pk},\\n1677:                 )\\n1678:         qs = self.queryset.filter(**{\\\"%s__in\\\" % key: value})\\n1679:         pks = {str(getattr(o, key)) for o in qs}\\n1680:         for val in value:\\n1681:             if str(val) not in pks:\\n1682:                 raise ValidationError(\\n1683:                     self.error_messages[\\\"invalid_choice\\\"],\\n1684:                     code=\\\"invalid_choice\\\",\\n1685:                     params={\\\"value\\\": val},\\n1686:                 )\\n1687:         return qs\\n1688: \\n1689:     def prepare_value(self, value):\\n1690:         if (\\n1691:             hasattr(value, \\\"__iter__\\\")\\n1692:             and not isinstance(value, str)\\n1693:             and not hasattr(value, \\\"_meta\\\")\\n1694:         ):\\n1695:             prepare_value = super().prepare_value\\n1696:             return [prepare_value(v) for v in value]\\n1697:         return super().prepare_value(value)\\n1698: \\n1699:     def has_changed(self, initial, data):\\n1700:         if self.disabled:\\n1701:             return False\\n1702:         if initial is None:\\n1703:             initial = []\\n1704:         if data is None:\\n1705:             data = []\\n1706:         if len(initial) != len(data):\\n1707:             return True\\n1708:         initial_set = {str(value) for value in self.prepare_value(initial)}\\n1709:         data_set = {str(value) for value in data}\\n1710:         return data_set != initial_set\\n1711: \\n1712: \\n1713: def modelform_defines_fields(form_class):\\n1714:     return hasattr(form_class, \\\"_meta\\\") and (\\n1715:         form_class._meta.fields is not None or form_class._meta.exclude is not None\\n1716:     )\\n1717: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/forms/models.py\"}"
